lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: To the assemblies.
meanings.nirukta: From 'saha' (together) + √bhā (to shine). A place where people sit together and shine through mutual wisdom and order.
meanings.vedantic: The assembly represents the collective consciousness. The Lord is the self of the assembly, present in the collective social order and governance.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem सभा + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for assembly or congregation (sabhā-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under assembly names in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the lords of the assemblies / presidents.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'sabhā' (assembly) + 'pati' (lord). He who presides over, directs, and protects the congregation.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the supreme intelligence that guides all assemblies and councils, maintaining justice, harmony, and cosmic law (Rta).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem सभापति + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Related to rulers and presidents in Ksatriyavarga.

meanings.english: To the horses / those who lack personal property (a-sva).
meanings.nirukta: From √aś (अशूँ व्याप्तौ, Kryādi-gaṇa, 9.0062) meaning to pervade or travel fast. Etymologized by Yāska as 'aśuvyāpī' (fast-pervading). Alternatively parsed as 'a-sva' (having no property, representing ascetics).
meanings.vedantic: The horse represents the senses in the Upanishads (indriyāṇi hayān āhuḥ). The Lord is the self of the senses, and also the self of the poor and property-less (a-sva).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem अश्व + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for horse (aśva-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.14).
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Catur-aṅga-kāṇḍa (3.24) under horse names.

meanings.english: To the lords of horses.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'aśva' (horse) + 'pati' (lord). One who controls, owns, or cares for horses. Also represents the wealthy.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the controller of the senses (the horses of the chariot of the body). He is the supreme chariot-driver (Sarathi) of our lives.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem अश्वपति + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with horse-keepers and cavalry leaders.

meanings.english: To the distinguished chief / peak / outstanding one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √kak (कक लौल्ये, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0886) or associated with heights/peaks. Refers to the foremost or most prominent.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the Lord as the highest peak of reality, the supreme peak among all deities and the sovereign lord of all creation.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem ककुभ + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The 'ṅe' suffix is replaced by 'ya' after an 'a'-ending nominal stem by 'Geryaḥ' (P. 7.1.13), and the preceding 'a' is lengthened by 'Ato dīrgho yañi' (P. 7.3.101), yielding 'क॒कु॒भाय॑'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for direction (diś-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.6) representing the cardinal peaks.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.58) as direction or peak: 'kakup kakubho vā'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under directions and peaks in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the one with a sword / one with a quiver.
meanings.nirukta: Prefix 'ni' + √sañj (षञ्जँ सङ्गे, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1147) + suffix 'ini'. One who has a sword attached to his waist or holds a quiver.
meanings.vedantic: Symbolizes the Lord who is constantly equipped with the weapon of discrimination to cut through the false attachments of the seeker.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem निषङ्गिन् + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). For '-in', '-i', '-u' or consonant stems, appropriate guna or sandhi rules apply. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with swords and weapons in Naighaṇṭuka-kāṇḍa.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.61) as sword-bearer or archer.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under weapon holders in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: Of the secret thieves.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √sten (स्तेनँ चौर्ये, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0577). Yāska (Nirukta) defines 'stena' as one who commits theft secretly (stenaḥ saṃstyanād vā, steyād vā).
meanings.vedantic: Salutation to the Lord as the lord of secret thieves. This reveals the all-pervasive nature of the Divine (Sarvātmatva), who exists even in the outcast and the sinner. It teaches the seeker to look past social judgments and see the singular consciousness in all.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem स्तेन + the genitive plural case-ending 'ām' (ṣaṣṭhī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The augment 'nuṭ' is added by 'Hrasvanadyāpo nuṭ' (P. 7.1.54) and vowel is lengthened by 'Nāmi' (P. 6.4.3), yielding final form.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for thieves (stena-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Śūdravarga (2.10.42): 'stena-vyāsa-parimoṣi-caura-taskarāḥ' (defining synonyms for thieves).

meanings.english: To the possessor of a quiver.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'iṣu' (arrow) + 'dhi' (container, from √dhā) + possessive suffix 'matup'. He who carries the arrow-holder.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the repository of all karmas and experiences. The Lord holds the quiver of cosmic results, discharging them as arrows of destiny.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem इषुधिमत् + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). For '-in', '-i', '-u' or consonant stems, appropriate guna or sandhi rules apply. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with weapon containers in Vedic texts.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under weapon accessories in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: Of the open thieves / highway robbers.
meanings.nirukta: Derived by Pāṇini as an irregular compound 'tat' + 'kara' with s-sūtram (P. 6.1.157). One who robs openly on paths, saying 'I will do that' (i.e. steal).
meanings.vedantic: Saluting the Lord in the form of open robbers. If the Lord is the sole reality, all actions (even those labeled as sinful by society) are part of His play. This destroys dualistic moral superiority in the seeker.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem तस्कर + the genitive plural case-ending 'ām' (ṣaṣṭhī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The augment 'nuṭ' is added by 'Hrasvanadyāpo nuṭ' (P. 7.1.54) and vowel is lengthened by 'Nāmi' (P. 6.4.3), yielding final form.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for thieves and robbers (stena-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Śūdravarga (2.10.42) as a synonym for thief.

meanings.english: To the deceiver / cheat.
meanings.nirukta: From √vañc (वञ्चु गत्योः, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0204 or in causative: to deceive). Present participle 'at' (śatṛ). One who cheats in trade or transactions.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is Maya, the ultimate power of delusion and deception that makes the impermanent appear permanent.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem वञ्चत् + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). For '-in', '-i', '-u' or consonant stems, appropriate guna or sandhi rules apply. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Related to terms for cheating (vañchana, pratāraṇa) in Vyāvahārikavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under deceptive agents in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the thorough deceiver / master cheat.
meanings.nirukta: Prefix 'pari' (completely) + root √vañc (to deceive) + present participle suffix. One who cheats systematically in all transactions.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the total grip of cosmic illusion (Mahamaya) that covers all aspects of the objective universe, binding the soul in complete duality.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem परिवञ्चत् + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). For '-in', '-i', '-u' or consonant stems, appropriate guna or sandhi rules apply. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with systematic deception and delusion.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under terms of deception in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: Of the sneaky pilferers.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root √stā (to steal/conceal) with the Uṇādi suffix 'yu'. Defined as an insider thief who steals from trusted associates.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord in the form of insider thieves who steal silently. It signifies that the thief is not other than the indwelling Self of all.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem स्तायु + the genitive plural case-ending 'ām' (ṣaṣṭhī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The augment 'nuṭ' is added by 'Hrasvanadyāpo nuṭ' (P. 7.1.54) and vowel is lengthened by 'Nāmi' (P. 6.4.3), yielding final form.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for thieves (stena-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with thieves and pilferers in Śūdravarga.

meanings.english: To the quiet wanderer / stealthy roamer.
meanings.nirukta: Prefix 'ni' (quietly/inwardly) + √car (चरँ गत्यर्थः, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0989) + Uṇādi suffix 'eru'. One who wanders silently with the intent to steal.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the silent witness (sakshi) who roams in the background of all thoughts, unnoticed by the outgoing mind.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem निचेरु + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). For '-in', '-i', '-u' or consonant stems, appropriate guna or sandhi rules apply. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with movement and wandering in Vedic texts.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Related to secret or stealthy movements in Avyayavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under movement terms in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the roamer around / attendant / servant.
meanings.nirukta: Prefix 'pari' (around) + √car (to wander) + suffix 'ac'. One who roams in public spaces, markets, or acts as an attendant.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the active and dynamic aspect of consciousness that operates in the outer marketplace of the senses, serving the cosmic order.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem परिचर + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The 'ṅe' suffix is replaced by 'ya' after an 'a'-ending nominal stem by 'Geryaḥ' (P. 7.1.13), and the preceding 'a' is lengthened by 'Ato dīrgho yañi' (P. 7.3.101), yielding 'प॒रि॒-च॒राय॑'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Noted under helper or servant synonyms in Vedic glossaries.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under servants and helpers in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: Of the forests / forest-dwellers / forest-thieves.
meanings.nirukta: From √ṛ (to go/obtain) + suffix 'anya'. Yāska defines it as a place remote from human habitation ('araṇyam apāram', Nirukta). Here refers to forest-thieves or dwellers.
meanings.vedantic: The forest represents the untamed mind, full of wild thoughts and thieves (passions). The Lord is the controller of this untamed nature.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem अरण्य + the genitive plural case-ending 'ām' (ṣaṣṭhī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The augment 'nuṭ' is added by 'Hrasvanadyāpo nuṭ' (P. 7.1.54) and vowel is lengthened by 'Nāmi' (P. 6.4.3), yielding final form.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for forest (araṇya-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.16).
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Vana-kāṇḍa (4.1) under forest names.

meanings.english: To the possessors of missiles / those armed with missiles.
meanings.nirukta: From 'sṛka' (missile, from √sṛj to release) + possessive suffix 'vin' (ini by P. 5.2.115). Those who protect themselves with mechanical throwing devices.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the weapons of cosmic destruction, showing that the physical instruments of warfare and death are forms of the Divine.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem सृकाविन् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: 'sṛka' is listed as a synonym for weapon or thunderbolt (vajra-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.20).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with weapons and arrows in Kṣatriyavarga.

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Amarakośa Avyayavarga (3.4.19) as a term indicating bowing, salutation, or reverence.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: Of the plunderers / stealers.
meanings.nirukta: From √muṣ (मुषँ स्तेये, Kryādi-gaṇa, 9.0065) + present participle suffix 'śatṛ'. One who robs or steals crops/grain from fields.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the stealer of the ego. Just as a grain-thief takes away the harvest, the Lord steals the crops of karmas from the devotee's mind.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem मुष्णत् + the genitive plural case-ending 'ām' (ṣaṣṭhī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The augment 'nuṭ' is added by 'Hrasvanadyāpo nuṭ' (P. 7.1.54) and vowel is lengthened by 'Nāmi' (P. 6.4.3), yielding final form.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for thieves (stena-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Śūdravarga (2.10.42) as thief or stealer.

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Amarakośa Avyayavarga (3.4.19) as a term indicating bowing, salutation, or reverence.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: Of the cutters / slashers.
meanings.nirukta: Prefix 'pra' + √kṛt (कृतीँ छेदने, Tudādi-gaṇa, 6.0006) + kta-pratyaya (used as agentive/noun). Those who cut down trees, slaughter animals, or slash purses.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the mower of life, cutting the thread of worldly attachment and dissolving the physical form at the ordained time.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem प्रकृन्त + the genitive plural case-ending 'ām' (ṣaṣṭhī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The augment 'nuṭ' is added by 'Hrasvanadyāpo nuṭ' (P. 7.1.54) and vowel is lengthened by 'Nāmi' (P. 6.4.3), yielding final form.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Related to terms for cutting or dividing (cchedana) in Vyāvahārikavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under cutting or violent actions in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the turban-wearer / king / reputable head.
meanings.nirukta: From 'uṣṇīṣa' (turban, head-dress) + possessive suffix 'ini'. Represents kings, chieftains, or respectable leaders who wear turbans.
meanings.vedantic: The turban symbolizes authority and respect. The Lord is the ultimate crown/turban of all rulers and respectable citizens, as well as the crown of knowledge.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem उष्णीषिन् + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). For '-in', '-i', '-u' or consonant stems, appropriate guna or sandhi rules apply. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with ornaments and headwear in Vedic texts.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under ornaments and crowns in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the mountain-roamer / mountain-wanderer.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'giri' (mountain) + √car (to wander/live). One who resides or roams in the high hills and caves.
meanings.vedantic: Points to the Lord who resides on Mount Kailasa, and also to the spiritual heights of consciousness (mountains) where the Self roams freely.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem गिरिचर + the dative singular case-ending 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The 'ṅe' suffix is replaced by 'ya' after an 'a'-ending nominal stem by 'Geryaḥ' (P. 7.1.13), and the preceding 'a' is lengthened by 'Ato dīrgho yañi' (P. 7.3.101), yielding 'गि॒रि॒-च॒राय॑'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Noted under mountain-dwelling deities or beings.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Related to foresters and mountain-dwellers in Vanavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Associated with mountains and residents in Bhūmi-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: Of the pluckers / pickpockets / house-thieves.
meanings.nirukta: From 'kula' (family/house/wealth) + √luñc (लुञ्चँ अपनयने, Tudādi-gaṇa, 6.0091) meaning to pluck or tear. Those who steal household goods or pick pockets.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the plucker of the fruits of action (karma-phala). He plucks away the weeds of vanity from the devotee's mind.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem कुलुञ्च + the genitive plural case-ending 'ām' (ṣaṣṭhī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). The augment 'nuṭ' is added by 'Hrasvanadyāpo nuṭ' (P. 7.1.54) and vowel is lengthened by 'Nāmi' (P. 6.4.3), yielding final form.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with thieves and pickpockets in Śūdravarga.

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Amarakośa Avyayavarga (3.4.19) as a term indicating bowing, salutation, or reverence.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: To the bow-bearers / those who carry bows.
meanings.nirukta: From 'dhanvan' (bow, from √dhanv to run/flow) + possessive suffix 'vin' (ini). Those who carry bows for warfare.
meanings.vedantic: The bow is Pinaka or the Pranava (Om) in the Upanishads (dhanur praṇavo). The Lord is the ultimate bow-bearer who launches the soul towards liberation.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem धन्वाविन् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

meanings.english: To the those who strike from all sides.
meanings.nirukta: See entry in Anuvakam 3. Prefix 'ā' + √vyadh + feminine suffix 'ṅīp'.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord's feminine energies of destruction that strike the ego and cleanse the soul from all directions.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem आव्याधिनी + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

meanings.english: To the those who strike in diverse ways.
meanings.nirukta: Prefix 'vi' (diverse/special) + √vyadh (to pierce) + present participle feminine suffix 'śatṛ + ṅīp'. Those who pierce and wound in varied ways.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the diverse, multifaceted modes of karmic correction. The Lord corrections come in various forms to suit each soul.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem विविध्यन्ती + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under active feminine forces in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the armies.
meanings.nirukta: From √si (to bind) + suffix 'na'. An organized body of soldiers bound by a common discipline and command.
meanings.vedantic: The army represents the collective protective forces of righteousness (Dharma). The Lord is the self of these collective forces.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem सेना + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for army or host (senā-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Catur-aṅga-kāṇḍa under army names.

meanings.english: To the commanders of the armies.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'senā' (army) + √nī (णीञ् प्रापणे, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1049) meaning to lead. The general or commander who leads the host.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the supreme leader of the armies of light (Devas) fighting against the darkness (Asuras) within the human heart.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem सेनानी + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Catur-aṅga-kāṇḍa under army commanders.

meanings.english: To the chariot-drivers / gatekeepers.
meanings.nirukta: From √kṣad (क्षद् संविभागे, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0964) + agentive suffix 'tṛc'. Refers to the charioteer (who drives), gatekeepers, or distributors of food/wealth.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the driver of the chariot of life, and the gatekeeper who controls access to the inner chamber of the heart.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem क्षत्तृ + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with servants or charioteers in Vedic glossaries.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.63) as sarathi (charioteer) or gatekeeper.

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Amarakośa Avyayavarga (3.4.19) as a term indicating bowing, salutation, or reverence.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: To the carpenters.
meanings.nirukta: From √takṣ (तक्षूँ त्वचीकरणे, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0747). One who cuts, fashions, and carves wood to build houses or furniture.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the supreme carpenter of the universe (Vishvakarman), who fashions the names and forms (nama-rupa) of the physical world.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem तक्षन् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under craftsmen or creators in Vedic registers.

meanings.english: To the chariot-makers / artisans.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'ratha' (chariot) + √kṛ (to make). A skilled artisan belonging to the mixed caste whose occupation is building chariots.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the chariot-maker, building the physical vehicles (bodies) of all living beings to allow them to experience their karmas.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem रथकार + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under occupational specialists in Vedic texts.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Śūdravarga (2.10.8) under artisans and mixed castes.

meanings.english: To the potters.
meanings.nirukta: From 'kula' (the family or heap of clay) + √al (to shape). The craftsman who works on the wheel to fashion clay vessels.
meanings.vedantic: The potter is the classic Upanishadic analogy for the material cause. The Lord is both the potter (efficient cause) and the clay (material cause) of the cosmos.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem कुलाल + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

meanings.english: To the blacksmiths / metal-artisans.
meanings.nirukta: From 'karman' (metal work/activity) + √ṛ (to go). The blacksmith or metalworker who melts and shapes iron and other metals.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the blacksmith who melts the rigid, cold hearts of devotees in the fire of devotion and shapes them into vessels of grace.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem कर्मार + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under metal workers in Vedic registers.

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Amarakośa Avyayavarga (3.4.19) as a term indicating bowing, salutation, or reverence.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: To the mountaineer hunters / outcaste hunters.
meanings.nirukta: From 'ni' (inwardly/down) + √sad (षद्लँ विशरणगत्यवसादनेषु, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0990). The non-Vedic tribal hunter who settles in forests.
meanings.vedantic: The Niṣāda represents the lowest social outcast. Saluting the Lord as Niṣāda teaches the seeker that the Divine exists equally in the highest priest and the lowest outcast.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem निषाद + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for outcasts or forest-tribes (Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under tribal foresters in Bhūmi-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Amarakośa Avyayavarga (3.4.19) as a term indicating bowing, salutation, or reverence.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: To the hunters / seekers of beasts.
meanings.nirukta: From 'mṛga' (deer/wild beast) + √yā (या प्रापणे, Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0044) with 'un' suffix. One who seeks and tracks wild beasts.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the Hunter (Kirata). He tracks down the wild thoughts (deers) that run amok in the forest of the human mind.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem मृगयु + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for hunter (lubdhaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).

meanings.english: To the dog-keepers / those who lead dogs.
meanings.nirukta: From 'śvan' (dog) + √nī (to lead). One who leads dogs on leash, typically for hunting or guarding.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the keeper of dogs. Symbolizes that even the handlers of impure or lower animals are instruments of the Divine order.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem श्वनि + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with hunters or dog-keepers in Vedic glossaries.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Śūdravarga under hunters and keepers.

meanings.english: To the dogs.
meanings.nirukta: From √śvi (to swell/grow) + suffix 'kan'. Yāska defines śvan as that which breathes fast or grows quickly ('śvasanād vā', Nirukta).
meanings.vedantic: The dog is considered ritually impure in Vedic tradition. Saluting the Lord as the dog (svabhyah) shows that the highest divinity resides in what is socially deemed lowest.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem श्वन् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Noted under animal registers in Vedic texts.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.42) under dog names: 'śvā kuraḥ'.

meanings.english: To the lords of dogs.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'śvan' (dog) + 'pati' (lord). He who keeps, feeds, or breeds dogs, representing the outcaste (Chandalas).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the master of dogs. Points to the unity of all life, teaching the seeker to recognize the Lord in both the dog and the handler.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem श्वपति + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Śūdravarga as outcaste or dog-handler.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under outcaste names in Bhūmi-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the members of fierce troops / fierce goddesses.
meanings.nirukta: From 'ud' (mighty/fierce) + 'gaṇa' (troop). Refers to the fierce bands of divine mothers (Saptamatrikas) or Rudra's female attendants.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord manifesting in the fierce, collective forces of nature and the powerful female deities that protect the cosmic order.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem उगणा + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with divine retinues in Vedic glossaries.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with the Matrikas and fierce goddesses in Svargavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under names of goddesses in Svarga-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the slayers / destroying ones.
meanings.nirukta: From √tṛh (तृंहँ हिंसार्थः, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0847 or Kryādi-gaṇa, 9.0069) + present participle feminine 'śatṛ + ṅīp'. Those who crush or destroy.
meanings.vedantic: The force of absolute dissolution. The Lord crushes the illusions, fears, and sins of His devotees, returning them to pure peace.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem तृंहती + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with killing or crushing in Vedic texts.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Related to terms for injury or killing in Manuṣyavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under violent actions in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the wise counsellors / sharp-minded ones.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √gṛdh (गृधुँ अभिकाङ्क्षायाम्, Divādi-gaṇa, 4.0152) or √gṛ (to praise). Commentators define 'gṛtsa' as wise, clever, or covetous.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the intellect (Buddhi) in the wise, and also as the desire/covetousness in ordinary beings, showing His presence in all mental faculties.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem गृत्स + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for wise or intelligent (medhāvi-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 3.15).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vyāvahārikavarga under intelligent names: 'gṛtso medhāvī'.

meanings.english: To the lords of the wise.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'gṛtsa' (wise/covetous) + 'pati' (lord). He who leads and protects the assemblies of wise counsellors.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the supreme director of the intellect (Preraka), guiding the minds of the wise towards right judgment and liberation.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem गृत्सपति + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with leaders of councils and wise groups.

meanings.english: To the members of bands / diverse occupational groups.
meanings.nirukta: From √vṛ (वृञ् वरणे, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1040). Yāska defines 'vrāta' as a group of people of different families and occupations who move together.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord resides in the diverse, unstructured groups of society, including wandering bands and nomadic communities, indicating His presence in all classes.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem व्रात + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for group or troop (vrāta-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under groups and crowds in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the lords of the bands.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'vrāta' (band/group) + 'pati' (lord). The leader of a group of outcasts or nomadic workers.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the protector and guide of those who live on the fringes of society, demonstrating that no group is outside the divine light.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem व्रातपति + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with group leaders and tribal chiefs.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under group leaders in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the members of the retinues / Shiva's troops.
meanings.nirukta: From √gaṇ (गणँ संख्याने, Churādi-gaṇa, 10.0381). A counted group or troop, specifically the demigod hosts of Shiva.
meanings.vedantic: The 'Gaṇas' represent the classified forces of nature. The Lord is present as every individual member of these cosmic forces.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem गण + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for group or assembly (gaṇa-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.8) as Shiva's host: 'pramathās tu gaṇāḥ'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under Shiva's host in Svarga-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the lords of the retinues / Ganeshas.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'gaṇa' (troop) + 'pati' (lord). The leader of Shiva's divine hosts (Pramatha-ganas), representing Lord Ganesha.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the master of all categories (Gaṇas) of existence, the remover of obstacles (Vighneshvara), and the gateway to knowledge.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem गणपति + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Svarga-kāṇḍa (1.37) under names of Ganesha: 'gaṇādhipo gaṇapatiḥ'.

meanings.english: To the misshapen ones / those of diverse forms.
meanings.nirukta: Prefix 'vi' (deformed/contrary or diverse) + 'rūpa' (form). One who has unusual, grotesque, or diverse physical features.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in the deformed, the disabled, and the grotesque. Beauty and ugliness are both relative projections; the Divine underlies both.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem विरूप + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Related to terms for deformity in Manuṣyavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under forms and shapes in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the universal-formed ones / those of all shapes.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'viśva' (all/universe) + 'rūpa' (form). He whose body is the entire universe, containing all shapes and objects.
meanings.vedantic: The Visvarupa (Universal Form) of the Lord, as described in the Gita (Chapter 11). All individual forms are parts of this one cosmic body.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem विश्वरूप + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for the universe or sun in Vedic registers.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.20) as a name of the Supreme Lord or Vishnu.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under names of the Supreme in Svarga-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the salutations.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring (namanīya).
meanings.vedantic: Interpreted as 'Na' (not) + 'mama' (mine) — indicating the complete surrender of the individual ego and the realization that nothing belongs to the limited self, but all belongs to the Supreme consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem नमस् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (udaka-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 1.12, item 17) and food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7) due to its Vedic polysemous usage representing nourishment and flow.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Amarakośa Avyayavarga (3.4.19) as a term indicating bowing, salutation, or reverence.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Anekārtha-kāṇḍa (5.26) under Avyayas, meaning bowing or salutation (namaskāra).

meanings.english: To the small ones / minor ones.
meanings.nirukta: From √kṣud (to crush, make small) + suffix 'ka'. That which is tiny, insignificant, or minor.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in the smallest atom (aṇor aṇīyān) as well as the greatest (mahato mahīyān). The insignificant is also Divine.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem क्षुल्लक + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for small or minor (hrasva-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vyāvahārikavarga (3.1) as tiny or minor: 'kṣullakaḥ kṣudraḥ'.

meanings.english: To the chariot-riders / those who own chariots.
meanings.nirukta: From 'ratha' (chariot) + possessive suffix 'ini' (in). One who rides in or owns a chariot.
meanings.vedantic: The chariot represents the body. The 'Rathin' is the individual self (Jivatman) riding in the chariot of the body, guided by the intellect.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem रथिन् + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with speed and chariot-warriors in Vedic registers.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.63) as chariot-rider or warrior.

meanings.english: To the those without chariots / pedestrians.
meanings.nirukta: Negative prefix 'a' (not) + 'ratha' (chariot). One who has no chariot and walks on foot.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in both the wealthy chariot-riders and the poor pedestrians, establishing absolute equality (samatva) in the Divine vision.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem अरथ + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with foot-travelers and pedestrians in Kṣatriyavarga.

meanings.english: To the chariots / vehicle forms.
meanings.nirukta: From √ramh (to speed) or √ṛ (to go) + suffix 'tha'. Yāska defines ratha as that which speeds ('ramhana-shila', Nirukta). Represents vehicles.
meanings.vedantic: The chariot is the physical body (rathas tu śarīram). Saluting the vehicle itself shows that the inert matter of the body is also a divine temple.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem रथ + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for vehicle (ratha-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu).
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Catur-aṅga-kāṇḍa (3.20) under chariot names.

meanings.english: To the lords of chariots.
meanings.nirukta: Compound of 'ratha' (chariot) + 'pati' (lord). He who owns, directs, or commands a fleet of chariots.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the controller of the cosmic vehicles (stars, planets, and bodies) that move according to His laws.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the nominal stem रथपति + the dative plural case-ending 'bhyas' (caturthī-bahuvacana) by 'Svaujasmauṭchasta...' (P. 4.1.2). By P. 8.2.7 (na-lopaḥ prātipadikāntasya) if stem ends in 'n', the 'n' is dropped before 'bhyas'. Governed by 'Namaḥ-svasti...' (P. 2.3.16) in relation to the word 'namaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with chariot-owners and kings in Kṣatriyavarga.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified under vehicle masters in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa.

meanings.english: To the source of existence / the creator / the one who exists as the universe.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'bhū' (भू) meaning 'to be or become'. 'bhavaty asmāt jagad iti bhavaḥ' (He from whom the universe arises).
meanings.vedantic: The ultimate source of all creation, representing the aspect of Brahman that manifests as the living universe (Sṛṣṭi-kartā).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √bhū (भू सत्तायाम्, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0001) with the suffix 'ghañ' (घञ्) or 'ap' (अप्) in the sense of agent. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms of existence or birth.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.31) as a synonym of Lord Śiva: 'śambhur īśaḥ paśupatiḥ śivo bhavaḥ'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Devatā-kāṇḍa (1.25) under synonyms of Śiva.

meanings.english: To the ancient / the old.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'vṛdh' (वृध्) meaning 'to grow, increase'.
meanings.vedantic: The eternal, primeval Puruṣa (Sanātana) who existed before anything else was created.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √vṛdh (वृधँ वृद्धौ, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0964) with the past passive participle suffix 'kta'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for aged or grown.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Manuṣyavarga (2.6.46) under age terms: 'वृद्धो जीर्णो वयोऽधिकः'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under terms for old age.

meanings.english: To the first-born / the primordial one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'agra' (beginning/forefront) + suffix 'gha' (इय). 'agre bhavaḥ agriyaḥ' (He who exists at the very beginning).
meanings.vedantic: The primordial cause of the universe (Kāraṇa-puruṣa) who exists prior to the manifestation of Prakṛti.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'agra' with the suffix 'ghach' (इय) by Aṣṭādhyāyī 4.4.117. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for beginning or priority.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga under terms for forefront/first.

meanings.english: To the first / the foremost.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'prath' (प्रथ्) meaning 'to spread, extend, or be first'.
meanings.vedantic: The first manifest form of Brahman (Hiraṇyagarbha/Sūtrātman) who spreads and expands the universe.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √prath (प्रथँ प्रख्याने, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0872) + suffix 'amack' (अमच्) or similar primary derivation. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for number one or beginning.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga/Nānārthavarga as a term for first: 'ādiḥ prathamaḥ'.

meanings.english: To the swift / the all-pervading.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'aś' (अश्) meaning 'to pervade, reach, or occupy'.
meanings.vedantic: The all-pervading consciousness that reaches everywhere instantly, traversing all space without movement.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √aś (अशूँ व्याप्तौ, Svādi-gaṇa, 5.0022) with the Uṇādi suffix 'u' (कुन्). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for quick/swift (kṣipra-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.15).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga (3.4.17) under quickness: 'āśu kṣipraṃ drutaṃ tvaritam'.

meanings.english: To the rapid / agile / active.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'aj' (अज्) meaning 'to drive, throw, or move'.
meanings.vedantic: The dynamic aspect of Brahman (Prāṇa) that is ever-active, initiating all movement and life in the universe.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √aj (अजँ गतिक्षेपणयोः, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0268) with the suffix 'kirach' (किरच्) by Uṇādi 1.53. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for quick/swift (kṣipra-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.15).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga under speed/quickness: 'tvaritaṃ tarasā’jiram'.

meanings.english: To the one in swift-flowing waters.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'śīghra' (swift) + suffix 'ya' or 'iya'. He who exists in rapid water currents.
meanings.vedantic: The presence of Brahman in the dynamic, fast-flowing aspects of nature, representing the unstoppable flow of time and life.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'śīghra' (fast) with the suffix 'gha' (इय) or 'yat' (य) in the sense of location/source. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for a swift-moving entity or water current.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under speed and water synonyms.

meanings.english: To the one in water-currents / rapids.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'śībha' meaning swift flow or water rapids. He who dwells in water currents.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the underlying life-force in the refreshing and purifying flow of water.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'śībha' (swift water current) with the suffix 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for water flow or currents.

meanings.english: To the one in the waves.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'ūrmi' (wave) + suffix 'ya'. 'ūrmiṣu bhavaḥ ūrmyaḥ' (He who exists in the waves).
meanings.vedantic: The waves represent the constant modifications (vṛttis) of the mind or the rising and falling of cycles of creation. Brahman is the ocean that supports these waves.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'ūrmi' (wave) + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 4.4.110. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūvarga (2.3.5) under wave synonyms: 'तरङ्ग ऊर्मिर्द्वयोः'.

meanings.english: To the one in quiet / still waters.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'ava' (down/under/without) + 'svana' (noise) + suffix 'ya'. 'avasvane (soundless water) bhavaḥ' (He who exists in silent water).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the silent, unperturbed state of consciousness (Turiya) which is free from the noise of thoughts and action.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from prefix 'ava' + 'svana' (noise) + suffix 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under quiet water or depth synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Associated with silent or deep water.

meanings.english: To the one in the streams.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'srotas' (stream/current) + suffix 'ya'. 'srotasi bhavaḥ srotasyaḥ' (He who exists in flowing streams).
meanings.vedantic: The streams represent the continuous flow of divine grace and knowledge that purifies the devotee's intellect.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'srotas' (current) with the suffix 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under river or current synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūvarga under river-currents: 'स्रोतो वहा रैहणी'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under river and water flows.

meanings.english: To the one on the islands.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'dvīpa' (island, land surrounded by water) + suffix 'ya'. 'dvīpe bhavaḥ dvīpyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: The island represents the firm ground of truth (Satyaloka) in the middle of the ocean of saṃsāra. Brahman is that sanctuary.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'dvīpa' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 4.3.10. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūvarga under land divisions: 'द्वीपोऽस्त्रियामन्तरीपम्'.

meanings.english: To the destroyer / the one who withdraws the universe.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'śṛ' (शॄ) meaning 'to injure, break, or destroy'. 'śrṛṇāti hinaṣti sarvam iti śarvaḥ' (He who dissolves everything).
meanings.vedantic: The power of dissolution (Saṃhāra-śakti) that returns all names and forms back to the unmanifest state.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √śṝ (शॄ हिंसायाम्, Kryādi-gaṇa, 9.0030) with the suffix 'van' (वन्) or 'a' (अच्). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under deities or names of dissolution.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Devatā-kāṇḍa (1.26) as a synonym of Śiva.

meanings.english: To the Lord of bound souls (cattle).
meanings.nirukta: 'paśūnām patiḥ' (the master of the bound creatures/beasts). 'paśu' represents the bound soul; 'pati' represents the Lord/liberator.
meanings.vedantic: The Supreme Lord who controls the cycle of bondage (pāśa) and liberation (mokṣa) of individual souls (jīvas, referred to as paśus).

grammatical_references.panini: Compound (ṣaṣṭhī-tatpuruṣa) of 'paśu' (derived from √paś 'to bind') and 'pati' (derived from √pā 'to protect'). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Deity associated with protection of cattle/beings.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.31) as a synonym of Lord Śiva: 'paśupatiḥ śivo bhavaḥ'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Devatā-kāṇḍa (1.27) as a name of Śiva.

meanings.english: To the white-throated one / dark-throated one.
meanings.nirukta: 'śitiḥ kaṇṭho yasya saḥ'. 'śiti' can mean either white (pre-poison throat) or dark (from the poison).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the coexistence of the pure, untouched state of Brahman (white) and the manifest suffering of the world (dark/poison) held in absolute harmony.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound of 'śiti' (white/dark) and 'kaṇṭha' (throat). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with deities having colored attributes.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.30) as a name of Śiva: 'nīlakaṇṭhaḥ sitikaṇṭhaḥ'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Devatā-kāṇḍa (1.28) under names of Śiva.

meanings.english: To the one with matted hair.
meanings.nirukta: 'kapardaḥ asya asti iti kapardī' (He who has matted locks/hair).
meanings.vedantic: Matted locks represent the wild, unmanifested energy of nature (Prakṛti) held in control and discipline by the Supreme Spirit (Puruṣa).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'kaparda' (matted hair) with the possessive suffix 'ini' (इनि) by Aṣṭādhyāyī 5.2.115. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with specific Vedic hair attributes.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.30) as a name of Śiva: 'kapardī dhūrjaṭir jaṭilaḥ'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Devatā-kāṇḍa (1.29) under names of Śiva.

meanings.english: To the one with shaven hair.
meanings.nirukta: 'vyuptāḥ (shaven/cut) keśāḥ yasya saḥ' (He whose hair is shaven, representing the Sannyasi/renunciant).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the supreme ascetic aspect of Brahman, the lord of renunciation (Sannyasa) and teacher of Brahma-vidyā.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound of 'vyupta' (past passive participle of √vap 'to shear/shave') and 'keśa' (hair). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed under terms for shaven or ascetic states.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under names of ascetics and renunciants.

meanings.english: To the one with a hundred bows.
meanings.nirukta: 'śatam dhanvāni yasya saḥ' (He who possesses a hundred/countless bows to protect His devotees and destroy evil).
meanings.vedantic: The infinite power of protection (Rākṣasaka-śakti) and the multiplicity of divine instruments used to restore Dharma.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound of 'śata' (hundred) and 'dhanvan' (bow). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Epithet of a deity holding many bows.

meanings.english: To the one who dwells on the mountain / Kailasa.
meanings.nirukta: 'girau (on the mountain) śete (resides) iti giriśaḥ' (He who rests on Kailasa) or 'giriśa' meaning lord of speech/mountains.
meanings.vedantic: The mountain represents the height of spiritual consciousness (Kailasa). Dwelling there means residing at the peak of self-realization.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'giri' (mountain) + root √śī (शीड् स्वप्ने) with the 'da' suffix, or with possessive suffix 'śa' in lomādi-gaṇa. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Devatā-kāṇḍa (1.29) under names of Śiva.

meanings.english: To the one pervaded by rays / the form of Viṣṇu.
meanings.nirukta: 'śipi' means rays or cattle; 'viṣṭa' means entered. 'He who enters into the rays of the Sun' or 'He who enters into cattle as the inner ruler'.
meanings.vedantic: The all-pervading consciousness (Antaryāmin) that enters into every living entity and natural element (like sunbeams) to support them.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'śipi' (rays/cattle) + 'viṣṭa' (past passive participle of √viś 'to enter'). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for the Sun or Vishnu.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.20) as a name of Viṣṇu: 'kṣipiviṣṭas-tu viṣṇuḥ'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Devatā-kāṇḍa as a name of Viṣṇu/Śiva.

meanings.english: To the holder of arrows.
meanings.nirukta: 'iṣavaḥ (arrows) asya santi iti iṣumān' (He who possesses arrows).
meanings.vedantic: Arrows represent the active, directed powers of will (iccha-śakti) and action (kriyā-śakti) that target ignorance and restore cosmic order.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'iṣu' (arrow) with the possessive suffix 'matup'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for an archer or weapon-bearing deity.

meanings.english: To the small / short one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'hras' (ह्रस्) meaning 'to decrease or become small'.
meanings.vedantic: The small one, representing the Dahara-ākāśa — the tiny, subtle space of the heart where the infinite Brahman is meditated upon.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √hras (ह्रसँ अपचये, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0763) + 'ac' suffix. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under size or quantity synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga under terms for smallness.

meanings.english: To the dwarf / the form of Gaṇapati or Viṣṇu's dwarf incarnation.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'vam' (वम्) 'to spit/emit' or associated with 'vama' (beautiful/dwarf). He who is small of stature.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the Divine who resides as the dwarf (Vamana) in the center of the body, worshipped by all senses (as in Katha Upanishad: 'madhye vāmanam āsīnam...').

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun/adjective 'vamana'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga/Manuṣyavarga under dwarf synonyms.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under names of Viṣṇu or short-stature beings.

meanings.english: To the immeasurably vast / the great.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'bṛh' (बृह्) 'to grow, expand, or increase'.
meanings.vedantic: The infinite, all-pervading Brahman, free from all limitations of space, time, and object (desha-kala-vastu pariccheda rahita).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √bṛh (बृहँ वृद्धौ, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0256) with the present participle suffix 'śatṛ' or Uṇādi suffix. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for greatness or atmosphere.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga/Nānārthavarga under synonyms for large or great.

meanings.english: To the most excellent / senior in qualities / elder.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'vṛdha' (grown/elder) + 'īyasu' (comparative suffix). The elder or senior in age and quality.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the most ancient, full of infinite, supreme divine attributes (Guṇa-varṣīyas).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the base 'vṛdha' with the comparative suffix 'īyasun', where 'vṛdha' is replaced by 'varṣa' by Aṣṭādhyāyī 5.3.61. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under senior or grown synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga under terms for elder/growth.

meanings.english: To the eldest / the most excellent.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'praśasya' (praiseworthy) + superlative suffix 'iṣṭhan'. The eldest or most outstanding.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the most ancient, preceding all created beings; also the highest in excellence and praise.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'praśasya' with the suffix 'iṣṭhan', where 'praśasya' is replaced by 'jya' by Aṣṭādhyāyī 5.3.61. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for greatness or seniority.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Manuṣyavarga (2.6.45) as a synonym for elder: 'ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठो वयोऽधिकः'.

meanings.english: To the youngest / the smallest.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'yuvan' (young) or 'alpa' (small) + superlative suffix 'iṣṭhan'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the subtlest of the subtle (aṇor aṇīyān), residing in the smallest atom or the youngest child.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'yuvan' or 'alpa' with 'iṣṭhan', replaced by 'kan' by Aṣṭādhyāyī 5.3.64. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under smallness or youth synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Manuṣyavarga as a term for youngest: 'कनिष्ठस्तु यवीयसि'.

meanings.english: To the sound / fame.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'śru' (श्रु) 'to hear'. That which is heard (sound), or the fame that is spread.
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic sound (Nāda-brahman/OM) which is the first vibration of creation; also the supreme fame of the Lord.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √śru (श्रु  श्रवणे, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1091) with the suffix 'ap' (अप्) or 'ghañ'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for ear or sound.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga under terms for fame or hearing.

meanings.english: To the echo / response.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'prati' + root 'śru' (to hear). That which responds, i.e., the echo or reply.
meanings.vedantic: The responsiveness of the Divine, who always echoes the devotee's prayer and answers their call.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from prefix 'prati' + √śru + suffix 'ap'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with echo or responsive sounds.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga/Nānārthavarga under response or echo.

meanings.english: To the one with a swift army.
meanings.nirukta: 'āśu' (swift) + 'senā' (army) = 'āśu-ṣeṇa'. He whose hosts move with lightning speed.
meanings.vedantic: The hosts of the Lord (the Maruts, Pramathas) represent the forces of nature and thoughts that execute the divine will instantly.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound of 'āśu' and 'senā', with sandhi conversion of 's' to 'ṣ' by Aṣṭadhyāyī rules. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for a commander with a fast army.

meanings.english: To the one with a swift chariot.
meanings.nirukta: 'āśu' (swift) + 'ratha' (chariot) = 'āśu-ratha'. He whose chariot is fast-moving.
meanings.vedantic: The chariot represents the mind or the cosmos. The Lord's swift chariot symbolizes His rapid response to the devotee's call.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound of 'āśu' and 'ratha'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Epithet of a deity riding a swift chariot.

meanings.english: To the hero / warrior.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'śūr' (शूर्) meaning 'to be bold, powerful, or brave'.
meanings.vedantic: The ultimate courage (Shurya) that destroys the fear of death and the demons of ignorance and ego.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √śūr (शूरँ विक्रान्ते, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0664) with the 'ac' suffix. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for strength or courage (balam, Nighaṇṭu 2.9).
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.1) as a synonym for warrior: 'शूरो वीरश्च विक्रान्तः'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa (2.115) under brave warriors.

meanings.english: To the one who breaks through / pierces (the enemy forces).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'ava' + root 'bhid' (to break/split). 'avabhindati iti avabhindan' (He who pierces through obstacles/enemies).
meanings.vedantic: Brahman who breaks through the hard shell of ignorance (ajnana-kosha) to reveal the light of the Self.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from prefix 'ava' + √bhid (भिदँ विदारणे, Rudhādi-gaṇa, 7.0002) with the present participle suffix 'snatṛ'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Associated with destructive or piercing powers.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed under terms for breaking or splitting.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under verbs of piercing and destruction.

meanings.english: To the wearer of a breastplate / armor.
meanings.nirukta: 'varma' (armor) + suffix 'in' (इनि). He who is equipped with protective armor.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord who is His own armor, representing the supreme protection that surrounds the devotee.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'varman' (armor) + possessive suffix 'ini' by Aṣṭādhyāyī 5.2.115. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.64) as a synonym for armor: 'वर्म दंशनमुच्यते... तद्वान् वर्मी'.

meanings.english: To the one with a shield / protective chariot shelter.
meanings.nirukta: 'varūtha' (protective cover/shelter) + suffix 'in'. He who has a protective shield or shelter.
meanings.vedantic: The Divine as the ultimate refuge (Sharanagati-sthana) that shelters the soul from all worldly afflictions.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'varūtha' + possessive suffix 'ini'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.56) under chariot parts: 'वरूथो रथगुप्तिः'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under chariot shields and refuges.

meanings.english: To the wearer of a helmet.
meanings.nirukta: 'bilma' (helmet/protective headgear) + suffix 'in'. He who wears a helmet.
meanings.vedantic: The helmet represents the protection of the intellect (Buddhi) by divine wisdom, shielding the mind from delusion.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'bilma' + possessive suffix 'ini'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Epithet of a deity wearing head armor.

meanings.english: To the wearer of a coat of mail / armor.
meanings.nirukta: 'kavaca' (coat of mail/corslet) + suffix 'in'. He who wears a coat of mail.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the spiritual armor of divine grace (Kavacham) that shields the devotee from all negative forces.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'kavaca' + possessive suffix 'ini'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.64) as a synonym for coat of mail: 'कवचोऽस्त्रियाम्'.

meanings.english: To the renowned / famous in the Vedas.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'śru' (to hear). That which is heard or renowned. 'śruti-prasiddhaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: The Supreme Brahman who is revealed and heard of only in the sacred Upanishads (Śrutis).
