grammatical_references.panini: Derived from √śru + past passive participle suffix 'kta'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for fame or voice.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga under renowned or heard: 'श्रुतः प्रसिद्धे शाब्दिते'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed under fame and Vedic hearing.

meanings.english: To the one with a famous army.
meanings.nirukta: 'śrutā' (famous/heard of in Shrutis) + 'senā' (army) = 'śruta-senā'. He whose army is famous.
meanings.vedantic: The forces of the Lord (sacred disciplines, virtues, and laws of nature) which are celebrated in the scriptures for maintaining world order.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound of 'śruta' and 'senā'. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for a commander whose armies are famous.

meanings.english: To the first-born / born in former times.
meanings.nirukta: 'pūrve jātaḥ iti pūrvajaḥ' (He who was born before all others, i.e., Hiranyagarbha at the dawn of creation).
meanings.vedantic: The primordial consciousness that manifests as the first cause of the universe (Adipuruṣa).

grammatical_references.panini: Upapada compound of 'pūrva' + root √jan (जनीँ प्रादुर्भावे, 4.0044) + suffix 'da' (ड). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for primordial creator or eldest sibling.

meanings.english: To the last-born / born in later times.
meanings.nirukta: 'apare (later/end) jātaḥ iti aparajaḥ' (He who manifests at the time of dissolution, or in later ages).
meanings.vedantic: The aspect of Brahman that manifests at the end of cycles (Kalagni-rudra) to dissolve the universe.

grammatical_references.panini: Upapada compound of 'apara' + root √jan + suffix 'da' (ड). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for younger sibling or later manifest deity.

meanings.english: To the middlemost.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'madhya' (middle) + suffix 'ma' (म).
meanings.vedantic: The indwelling ruler who exists in the middle of all states (sustenance/preservation) and in the heart of all beings.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'madhya' + suffix 'ma' by Aṣṭādhyāyī 5.3.8. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga (3.3.136) as a term for middle: 'मध्यमं मध्यस्थेऽपि'.

meanings.english: To the immature / youthful / one who does not speak proudly.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'apa' (away/under) + 'galbha' (mature/proud). He who is immature or humble.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the child-like, simple, and unpretentious form of the Divine (Bala-rupa), free from ego and pride.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound of 'apa' + 'galbha' (from √galbh 'to be bold/confident'). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under youth or growth synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Synonym for a youth or child-like state.

meanings.english: To the one in the rear / born from the hips.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'jaghana' (hips/rear) + suffix 'ya'. 'jaghane bhavaḥ jaghanyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman present in the lowliest, humblest, or trailing parts of creation (such as tail-ends of animals or outer boundaries of the universe).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'jaghana' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭādhyāyī 4.3.55. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under physical body parts or lowliness.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Manuṣyavarga/Nānārthavarga under hips or lowest rank.

meanings.english: To the one at the roots / depths.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'budhna' (root/depth/foundation) + suffix 'ya'. 'budhne bhavaḥ budhniyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the fundamental foundation (Adhara) supporting all trees, mountains, and the entire universe from below.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'budhna' + suffix 'gha' (इय) or 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under sky or root synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Epithet of a foundational or deep deity.

meanings.english: To the one in the human world / city of Gandharvas.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'sobha' (that which has both merit and sin, i.e., the human world, or the celestial city of Gandharvas) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: The Divine who resides in the human world (Samsara) guiding souls through both good and bad experiences.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'sobha' + suffix 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed under city or world synonyms.

meanings.english: To the one in the protective thread / amulet.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'pratisara' (protective amulet/thread tied around the wrist during rituals) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman who manifests as the invisible shield of protection (Rakṣā) in sacred threads and prayers.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'pratisara' + suffix 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed under ornaments or protective charms: 'प्रतिसरो रश्मिसङ्केतयोपि'.

meanings.english: To the one in Yama's realm / the south.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'yama' (god of death/control) + suffix 'ya'. 'yame bhavaḥ yāmyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman present as the law of justice, death, and control (Yama-dharma) that governs the cosmic order and the transition of souls.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'yama' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 4.3.10. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under deities of the southern region.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga as a term for south or death-associated.

meanings.english: To the one in the world of security / peace.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'kṣema' (security, preservation of what is acquired) + suffix 'ya'. 'kṣeme bhavaḥ kṣemyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the ultimate source of peace, security, and well-being (Yoga-kṣema) for all devotees.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'kṣema' + suffix 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for safety or peace.

meanings.english: To the one in the fertile fields / crops.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'urvarā' (fertile soil, crop-bearing land) + suffix 'ya'. 'urvarāyāṃ bhavaḥ urvaryah'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the fertility of the soil and the nourishing power in grains and crops (Annam).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'urvarā' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 4.2.82. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for fertile earth.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūvarga (2.1.6) under fertile soil: 'उर्वरा सर्वशस्याढ्या'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Bhūmi-kāṇḍa under soil types.

meanings.english: To the one in the threshing-floor.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'khala' (threshing floor where grains are separated) + suffix 'ya'. 'khale bhavaḥ khalyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the purifier who separates the essence (soul) from the husk (ego/body) in the threshing floor of saṃsāra.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'khala' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 4.2.45. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vaiśyavarga (2.9.15) under farming terms: 'खलं धान्यमर्दनस्थानम्'.

meanings.english: To the one praised in Vedic hymns.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'śloka' (verse/hymn/sound) + suffix 'ya'. Worthy of being praised.
meanings.vedantic: The Supreme Lord who is the ultimate subject of all Vedic hymns and prayers of praise.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'śloka' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 5.1.119. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for voice or praise.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga/Nānārthavarga under praise or fame.

meanings.english: To the one at the end of the Veda / final destination.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'avasāna' (boundary, end of a verse, or death/dissolution) + suffix 'ya'. 'avasāne bhavaḥ avasānyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the final destination of all life and the ultimate conclusion (Upanishads/Vedanta) of all Vedic studies.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from the noun 'avasāna' (from prefix 'ava' + √so 'to finish/end') + suffix 'yat' (य). Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under ending or limit synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga/Nānārthavarga under limit or death.

meanings.english: To the one in the forests.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'vana' (forest) + suffix 'ya'. 'vane bhavaḥ vanyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: The presence of Brahman in the natural, wild, and untouched beauty of the forests.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'vana' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 4.4.110. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under forest or water synonyms.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūvarga under forest synonyms: 'वनं विपिनमरण्यम्'.

meanings.english: To the one in the thickets / valleys.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'kakṣa' (shrubbery, forest-thicket, or dry forest) + suffix 'ya'. 'kakṣe bhavaḥ kakṣyaḥ'.
meanings.vedantic: Brahman as the protector hidden in the deep thickets and impenetrable places of the world.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'kakṣa' + suffix 'yat' (य) by Aṣṭadhyāyī 4.2.82. Dative singular.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under dry forest or secret places.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Nānārthavarga (3.3.5) as a term for forest-thicket: 'कक्षा गहाने शुष्कवने'.

meanings.english: To the one in the form of the war-drum (dundubhi).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'dundubhi', which is onomatopoeic from the sound 'dhum-dhum' of the drum, or from √dubh (to strike/sound) with reduplication.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the cosmic sound (Nada-Brahman) that reverberates through the universe. The war-drum represents the call to action, awakening, and the roar of the cosmic dissolution.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'dundubhi' + suffix 'ya' (signifying existence/relation, by 'tatra bhavaḥ') -> 'dundubhya' + dative singular 'ṅe' (caturthī-ekavacana) -> 'dundubhyāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Śabdādivarga (1.5.8) as a synonym for drum: 'bherī dundubhir-ānakaḥ'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Classified in Śabda-kāṇḍa under musical instruments.

meanings.english: To the one in the form of the drum-stick, or the sound produced by striking.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'ā' + root 'han' (to strike/kill) + suffix 'ya' (āhananya), meaning that which is used to strike, or the sound produced thereby.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is not only the instrument of sounding (the drum-stick) but also the struck sound itself, representing both cause and effect in the cosmic display.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from ā-√han (हनँ हिंसागत्योः, Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0002) with the kṛtya suffix 'nyat' or 'yat' (by 'āṅy-āhanaḥ' or general rules) + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'āhananyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in flowing rivers (nadī).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'nadī' (river, from √nad - to sound/roar) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the dynamic, flowing stream of life and time (saṃsāra-nadī), roaring with activity, yet ever pure.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'nadī' + suffix 'yat' (or 'yaḥ') + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'nādyāya'.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Bhūmi-kāṇḍa under river names.

meanings.english: To the one residing in small ponds, tanks, or reservoirs (veśanta).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'veśanta' (a small pool, from √viś - to enter, where water settles) + suffix 'a' or 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra resides in the small, localized bodies of water, representing the individual souls (jīvas) in whom the cosmic consciousness resides in miniature.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'veśanta' + suffix 'aṇ' or 'ya' -> 'vaiśanta' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'vaiśantāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vārivarga (1.10.15) under small ponds: 'veśantaḥ palvalam'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Bhūmi-kāṇḍa under small water bodies.

meanings.english: To the one residing in deep wells (kūpa).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'kūpa' (a well/hole, from √kup - to be agitated, or from kavi + √pā) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the deep, hidden water of self-knowledge inside the well of the body, which must be drawn out through the rope of devotion and bucket of effort.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'kūpa' + suffix 'yat' (by rule 'kūpād-yañ') -> 'kūpya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'kūpyāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vārivarga (1.10.28) under wells: 'kūpas-tu andhūḥ'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in ditches, pits, or holes in the ground (avaṭa).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'avaṭa' (a pit/hole) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Shows that the divine is present in the uneven, broken, and dark pits of existence, protecting the traveller even in falls.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'avaṭa' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'avaṭyāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vārivarga under pits or ditches: 'avaṭo vā'.

meanings.english: English meaning for वर्​ष्याय
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for वर्​ष्याय
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of वर्​ष्याय

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for वर्​ष्याय

meanings.english: English meaning for अवर्​ष्याय
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for अवर्​ष्याय
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of अवर्​ष्याय

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for अवर्​ष्याय

meanings.english: To the one residing in the clouds (megha).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'megha' (cloud, from √mih - to sprinkle/make wet) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the cloud of divine mercy that gathers in the sky of the mind, ready to shower the nectar of bliss.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'megha' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'meghyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the lightning (vidyut).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'vidyut' (lightning, from vi-√dyut - to shine/flash) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the lightning-flash of sudden illumination (sphoṭa/satori) that dispels the darkness of ignorance in a single instant.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'vidyut' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'vidyutyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the clear, unclouded sky (īdhrā).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'īdhrā' (clear weather/pure light, from √indh - to kindle/shine) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the clear, untainted, and pure sky of pure consciousness (cidākāśa), free from the clouds of thought (vṛttis).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'īdhrā' + suffix 'ghas' or 'yat' -> 'īdhriya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'īdhriyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the hot sun, sunshine, or heat (ātapa).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'ātapa' (heat of the sun, from ā-√tap - to heat/shine) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the heat of spiritual austerity (tapas) that purifies the soul, and the light of truth that shines brightly.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'ātapa' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'ātapyāya'.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Svarga-kāṇḍa under light and heat.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the wind, gale, or storm (vāta).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'vāta' (wind, from √vā - to blow) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the vital breath (prāṇa) that moves all living beings, and the powerful wind of change that sweeps away old constructs.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'vāta' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'vātyāya'.

lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Svarga-kāṇḍa under wind names.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the storm, hailstorm, or wind of dissolution (reṣma).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'reṣma' (a storm/destroying wind, from √riṣ - to injure/perish) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the force of dissolution (pralaya) that resolves the universe back into its unmanifest cause, clearing the path for new creation.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'reṣma' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'reष्मियाय'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the building site, homestead, or wealth in the form of property (vāstu).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'vāstu' (house/site, from √vas - to dwell) + suffix 'tavya' or 'yat' (vāstavya).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the substrate and foundation of all structures, the ground of being on which the superstructure of the universe is built.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'vāstu' + suffix 'yat' -> 'vāstavya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'vāstavyāya'.

meanings.english: To the guardian/protector (pā) of the homestead/site (vāstu).
meanings.nirukta: Upapada compound: 'vāstu pati' or 'vāstu pāti' (he who protects the site/house).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra as Vāstoṣpati is the guardian of the home, protecting the family, cattle, and peace of the household.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived as an Upapada compound: 'vāstu' + √pā (पा रक्षणे, Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0051) + suffix 'ka' or 'ḍa' -> 'vāstupa' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'vāstupāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūmivarga under house guardians.

meanings.english: To the examiner, discerner, or the one who grasps/strikes the enemy.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'pra' + root 'mṛś' (मृश् - to touch, consider, reflect, or grasp).
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the Lord as the inner witness (sākṣin) who examines and weighs the karma of all beings, or who penetrates the essence of all things.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from pra-√mṛś (मृशँ आमर्शने, Tudādi-gaṇa, 6.0150) with the suffix 'ghañ' or 'ac' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'pramṛśāya'.

meanings.english: To the messenger or envoy (especially in the form of Agni/Fire).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √dū (to go) or √du (to distress/burn) + 'tan' suffix; he who goes to convey messages.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra as Agni acts as the divine messenger carrying the offerings of human beings to the gods, linking the finite to the infinite.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √dū (दू उपतापे, Divādi-gaṇa, 4.0042) or √du (दु गत्यर्थः) with Uṇādi suffix 'tan' (तन्) -> 'dūta' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'dūtāya'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under the deities of the middle region or messengers.

meanings.english: To the sent one, the special envoy, or the one who is highly beneficial.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'pra' + root 'hi' (to send/promote) + past passive participle 'kta' -> 'prahita'.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the Lord sent/manifested in the world for the welfare of devotees, or the promptings of the inner controller (Antaryāmin).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from pra-√hi (हिँ गतौ वृद्धौ च, Svādi-gaṇa, 5.0011) with the suffix 'kta' (क्त) + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'prahitāya'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under terms for messengers or servants sent on a mission.

meanings.english: English meaning for तीक्ष्णइषवे
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for तीक्ष्णइषवे
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of तीक्ष्णइषवे

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for तीक्ष्णइषवे

meanings.english: To the one bearing weapons (āyudha).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'āyudha' (weapon: ā-√yudh) + possessive suffix 'in' (ini).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra's weapons are the active forces of cosmic regulation and correction, always ready to protect Dharma and dissolve adharma.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'āyudha' + possessive suffix 'ini' -> 'āyudhin' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'āyudhine'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga under terms for armed warriors.

meanings.english: English meaning for सुआयुधाय
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for सुआयुधाय
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of सुआयुधाय

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for सुआयुधाय

meanings.english: To the one possessing an excellent/beautiful (su) bow (dhanvan).
meanings.nirukta: Bahuvrīhi compound: 'śobhanaṃ dhanus yasya saḥ' (he whose bow is excellent/beautiful).
meanings.vedantic: The bow represents the sacred syllable OM (Pranava). A 'beautiful bow' means the chanting and meditation on OM is pure, clear, and leads straight to the target (Brahman).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived as a Bahuvrīhi compound of 'su' + 'dhanvan' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'sudhanvane'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Kṣatriyavarga (2.8.84) as a synonym for bow: 'sudhanvā'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the small pathways, lanes, or footpaths.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'srutyā' (footpath/lane, derived from √sru - to flow/run) + suffix 'yat' or 'ghañ' (existing in).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is present not only in grand places but also in the narrowest, most remote, and humble pathways of life.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'srutyā' + taddhita suffix 'yat' (in the sense of 'bhavaḥ' - existing there, by rule 'tatra bhavaḥ' 4.3.53) + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'srutyāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūmivarga (2.1.15) as a synonym for a footpath: 'srutyā'.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Sāmānya-kāṇḍa (2.164) as a footpath.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the highways, main roads, or path of Dharma.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'pathin' (path) + suffix 'ya' (pathyā - fit for the path, or highway).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the protector of those who travel the main highway of spiritual life (the Devayāna and Pitṛyāna, or the path of right action/Dharma).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'pathin' + suffix 'yat' (by rule 'patho yuttarapadavyabahāre' or general 'tatra bhavaḥ' rules) -> 'pathya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'pathyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in a well, pit, or dense/impenetrable forest (kāṭa).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'kaṭa' (a hole, well, or dense forest) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is present in the hidden, deep, and difficult-to-reach recesses of the heart (the cavern of the heart, guhā), as well as in life's most challenging crises.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'kaṭa' + suffix 'yat' (bhava-arthe) + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'kāṭyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in valleys, low-lying lands, or water-channels (nīpa).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'nīpa' (low-land or foot of a mountain) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra resides in the lowest, humblest places, showing that the divine is equally present in high peaks and low valleys.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'nīpa' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'nīpyāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Bhūmivarga under mountain-valleys or lowlands.

meanings.english: To the one residing in slush, mud, or marshy waters (sūda).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'sūda' (marsh/mud/well) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in the muddy, impure, and transitional elements of life, showing that nothing is outside the divine presence.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'sūda' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'sūdyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in large lakes, pools, or ponds (saras).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'saras' (lake, from √sṛ - to flow/glide) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the tranquil, deep consciousness resembled by a calm lake (saras), reflecting the light of truth clearly.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'saras' + suffix 'yat' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'sarasyāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vārivarga (1.10) under lakes and pools.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Listed in Bhūmi-kāṇḍa under water bodies.

meanings.english: To Soma (the moon-god or beverage), or to the one who is accompanied by Umā (sa + umā).
meanings.nirukta: (1) 'Sa-umā' = accompanied by Umā (the Divine Mother/Sakti). (2) Derived from √su (to press/extract) + 'man' suffix, meaning the Soma juice or Moon-god.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is always united with His supreme energy (Sakti/Umā), through whom He performs creation, preservation, and dissolution. Also, Soma represents the nectar of immortal bliss (ānanda).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'soma' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'somāya'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under atmospheric deities and plant synonyms.

meanings.english: English meaning for शम्कराय
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for शम्कराय
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of शम्कराय

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for शम्कराय

meanings.english: English meaning for मयःकराय
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for मयःकराय
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of मयःकराय

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for मयःकराय

meanings.english: To Śiva (the auspicious, peaceful, and stainless one).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √śī (to lie down, in whom all things rest) + suffix 'van' or 'ac'. (1) 'Śeti sarvaṃ jagat asmin' — He in whom the entire universe rests at dissolution. (2) 'Vaśi' (control) transposed becomes 'Śiva'.
meanings.vedantic: The non-dual, pure consciousness (Turiya) which is stainless, free from attributes, and the substratum of all reality.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √śī (शीङ् स्वप्ने, Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0026) with Uṇādi suffix 'van' -> 'śiva' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'śivāya'.

meanings.english: To the supremely, excessively auspicious/stainless one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'śiva' + comparative suffix 'tarap' -> 'śivatara' (more auspicious than all others).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the absolute goodness and purity that surpasses all relative standards of good and bad; He is the ultimate reality.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'śiva' + comparative suffix 'tarap' (तरप्) by rule 'dvivachana-vibhajyopapade...' -> 'śivatara' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'śivatarāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in holy fords, bathing places, or sacred rivers (tīrtha).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'tīrtha' (ford/crossing place, from √tṛ - to cross) + suffix 'ya' (existing in).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is present in the sacred places (tīrthas) that purify the mind and allow the devotee to cross the stream of worldly life.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'tīrtha' + suffix 'yat' (tatra bhavaḥ) -> 'tīrthya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'tīrthyāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vārivarga (1.10.13) under holy crossings: 'tīrthaṃ śāstrādhvarar-ṣiju...'.

meanings.english: To the one residing on the banks of rivers or canals (kūla).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'kūla' (bank/slope, from √kūl - to obstruct/enclose) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Worshipping Rudra on the banks represents the boundary between the manifest world and the unmanifest consciousness, where the transition happens.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'kūla' + suffix 'yat' -> 'kūlya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'kūlyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing on the far shore (pāra) of the river of saṃsāra.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'pāra' (far shore/limit, from √pṛ - to cross/protect) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the goal of liberation (Moksha) residing on the far shore of the ocean of ignorance.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'pāra' + suffix 'yat' -> 'pārya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'pāryāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Vārivarga under banks: 'pāraṃ tasyāstataḥ'.

meanings.english: To the one residing on the near shore (avāra) of the river of saṃsāra.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'avāra' (near shore) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the immanent presence who resides right here in the midst of worldly life (saṃsāra), granting desires and guiding the Jiva.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'avāra' + suffix 'yat' -> 'avārya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'avāryāya'.

meanings.english: To the one who carries across (taraṇa) the ocean of sins through excellent (pra) means.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'pra' + root 'tṛ' (to cross) + nominalizer 'lyuṭ' -> 'prataraṇa' (the act or instrument of crossing).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the divine savior who provides the ferry (bhakti/devotion) to cross the turbulent waters of worldly life.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from pra-√tṛ with the suffix 'lyuṭ' (अना) by rule 'lyuṭ ca' (3.3.115) -> 'prataraṇa' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'prataraṇāya'.

meanings.english: To the one who lifts up (uttaraṇa) out of the world of ignorance.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'ud' + root 'tṛ' (to cross/rise) + nominalizer 'lyuṭ' -> 'uttaraṇa'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the teacher (Guru) who delivers the final knowledge of identity (Brahma-jñāna), lifting the soul out of relative existence entirely.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from ud-√tṛ with suffix 'lyuṭ' -> 'uttaraṇa' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'uttaraṇāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in crossing places, shallows, or ferry points (ātāra).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from prefix 'आ' + root 'tṛ' (to cross) + suffix 'yat' -> 'ātārya' (existing at the ferry-crossing).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra stands at the junction points of life (birth, death, major choices), guiding the soul through transitions.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from ā-√tṛ + suffix 'yat' -> 'ātārya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'ātāryāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the deep water, whirlpools, or who is related to the individual soul (Jiva).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'alāda' (whirlpool or Jiva experiencing fruits) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the indwelling controller of the Jiva, who experiences the fruits of actions (karma-phala) and wanders in the whirlpool of life.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'alāda' + suffix 'yat' -> 'ālādya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'ālādyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the young green grass (śaṣpa) on riverbanks.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'śaṣpa' (young grass/sprout) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the tender, fresh aspect of nature, showing the divine beauty in the smallest blade of grass.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'śaṣpa' + suffix 'yat' -> 'śaṣpya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'śaṣpyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the foam (phena) of waters.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'phena' (foam, from √phiph/√phan - to sound/boil) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is present in the foam, representing the transient, insubstantial names and forms of the universe, which are but foam on the ocean of consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'phena' + suffix 'yat' -> 'pheny' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'phenyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the sands (sikatā).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'sikatā' (sand, from √sic - to sprinkle/pour) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the infinite multiplicity of the universe, like the grains of sand on a riverbank, all resting in Him.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'sikatā' + suffix 'yat' (by rule 'sikatābhyo ca') -> 'sikatya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'sikatyāya'.

meanings.english: To the one residing in the flowing current (pravāha) of the river.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'pravāha' (flow/current, from pra-√vah - to flow/carry) + suffix 'ya'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the dynamic, continuous stream of cosmic time and life, carrying all creatures in His flow.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from pra-√vah + suffix 'yat' -> 'pravāhya' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'pravāhyāya'.

meanings.english: To the copper-red one (at sunrise).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √tam (to choke/darken) + suffix 'ran', meaning the color of copper.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the visible solar form of Rudra at sunrise, indicating the beginning of cosmic manifestation.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √tam (तमुँ काङ्क्षायाम्, Divādi-gaṇa, 4.0099) with Uṇādi suffix 'ran' -> 'tāmra' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'tāmrāya'.

meanings.english: To the ruddy or pinkish-red one (after sunrise).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √ṛ (to move/go) + Uṇādi suffix 'unan' -> 'aruṇa' (the dawn or ruddy color).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra as the morning sun (aruṇa) dispelling physical and mental darkness, bringing activity and life.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √ṛ (ऋँ गतौ, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1086) with Uṇādi suffix 'unan' -> 'aruṇa' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'aruṇāya'.

meanings.english: English meaning for शङ्गाय
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for शङ्गाय
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of शङ्गाय

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for शङ्गाय

meanings.english: To the supreme, excellent, or fierce one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √uc (to be strong/gather) or √vac + Uṇādi suffix 'ran' -> 'ugra' (powerful/fierce).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the supreme sovereign (Ugra) who governs all cosmic forces, including Indra and Agni, who operate out of fear of Him.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √uc (उचीँ समवाये, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0253) with Uṇādi suffix 'ran' -> 'ugra' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'ugrāya'.

meanings.english: To the terrifying or awe-inspiring one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √bhī (to fear) with suffix 'mak' -> 'bhīma' (he who causes fear).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra represents the fearsome aspect of reality (death, change, decay) that shakes the soul out of its complacency and worldly attachment.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √bhī (भी᳚ भये, Juhotyādi-gaṇa, 3.0002) with Uṇādi suffix 'mak' -> 'bhīma' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'bhīmāya'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under synonyms for fear or strength.
lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga under names of Śiva: 'bhīmaḥ'.

meanings.english: To the one who slays the enemies standing directly in front of His devotee.
meanings.nirukta: Compound: 'agre vadhaḥ yasya' (he whose killing/weapon operates in front).
meanings.vedantic: Shows the immediate, direct protection of the Lord in times of active combat or spiritual crisis.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'agre' (locative of agra) + 'vadha' (from √vadh - to kill) + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'agrevadhāya'.

meanings.english: To the one who slays the enemies from a distance.
meanings.nirukta: Compound: 'dūre vadhaḥ yasya' (he whose killing/weapon operates from afar).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the preventive grace of the Lord, neutralizing obstacles and negative karma before they manifest near the devotee.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'dūre' (locative of dūra) + 'vadha' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'dūrevadhāya'.

meanings.english: To the slayer/destroyer.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √han (to kill) + agentive suffix 'tṛc'.
meanings.vedantic: Rudra as the ultimate agent of dissolution who slays all names and forms at the end of time, returning them to their unmanifest source.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √han (हनँ हिंसागत्योः, Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0002) with agentive suffix 'tṛc' (तृच्) -> 'hantṛ' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'hantre'.

meanings.english: To the excessive or ultimate slayer.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √han (to kill) + comparative suffix 'īyasun' (hanīyas).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the absolute destroyer of all, who destroys even the destroyers themselves (Yama, time) at the final dissolution (mahāpralaya).

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √han + comparative suffix 'īyasun' -> 'hanīyas' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'hanīyase'.

meanings.english: To the deliverer who carries souls across the ocean of saṃsāra (or the Pranava OM).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from √tṛ (to cross/carry over) + suffix 'ghañ' -> 'tāra' (that which causes crossing).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the saving knowledge (Tāraka-Brahman) in the form of OM that carries the departing soul across the ocean of birth and death.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √tṛ (तॄ प्लवनतरणयोः, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1124) with suffix 'ghañ' -> 'tāra' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'tārāya'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Avyayavarga or Nānārthavarga under terms of deliverance or sound.

meanings.english: To the source of peace, welfare, or happiness (śam).
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'śam' (welfare/peace) + root 'bhū' (to exist/become) + suffix 'ḍu' -> 'śambhu' (he from whom welfare arises).
meanings.vedantic: Rudra is the primary cause and source of the highest spiritual peace (mukti) and bliss.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from 'śam' + √bhū (भू सत्तायाम्, Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0001) + suffix 'ḍu' (by rule Uṇādi 1.138) -> 'śambhu' + dative singular 'ṅe' -> 'śambhave'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed in Svargavarga (1.1.30) as a major name of Śiva: 'śambhuryis-tryambakaḥ'.

meanings.english: English meaning for मयःभवे
meanings.nirukta: Etymological analysis for मयःभवे
meanings.vedantic: Vedantic usage of मयःभवे

grammatical_references.panini: Paninian root and rules for मयःभवे

meanings.english: To the one dwelling in barren/salty land.
meanings.nirukta: iriṇa + yat. irinamūṣaram, tatra bhavaḥ irinyaḥ (dwelling in barren or salty soil).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the Lord's presence even in barren, non-productive aspects of the universe, showing that no place is devoid of His divinity.

grammatical_references.panini: Stem 'iriṇya' formed with yat-pratyaya (P.4.3.53) on 'iriṇa'. Dative singular case suffix 'ṅe' replaced by 'yā' by 'ṅerinyā...' (P.7.1.13).

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under names of waste/dry land.
lexicographical_references.abhidhana_ratnamala: Bhūmi-kāṇḍa (2.12): Synonym for saline or barren land.

meanings.english: To the one who is in the main highway / well-trodden path.
meanings.nirukta: prapatha + yat. prapathe bhavaḥ prapathyaḥ (situated in the main path).
meanings.vedantic: Symbolizes the Lord as the guide on the royal road of spiritual progress (the path of knowledge or devotion).

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'pra' + noun 'pathin' with suffix 'yat' (P.4.3.53). Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in hard/uneven ground or paths.
meanings.nirukta: lōpa (broken path/rough ground) + yat. lope bhavaḥ lopyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Divinity exists in rough terrains and challenging circumstances.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'lōpa' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in wild grass/shrubs.
meanings.nirukta: ulapa (shrub/creeper/grass) + yat. ulape bhavaḥ ulapyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Present in tiny creepers and wild grasses, representing the humility of nature.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'ulapa' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated on the earth.
meanings.nirukta: urvī (earth) + yat. urvyāṃ bhavaḥ ūrvyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the support of the earth, manifesting as the terrestrial realm.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'urvī' + 'yat' suffix with Vedic lengthening. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one in the beautiful waves / hollow metal images.
meanings.nirukta: sūrmī (waves or metal image) + yat. sūrmyāṃ bhavaḥ sūrmyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in flowing waves and also inside hollow metal statues, showing He fills all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'sūrmī' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one in the leaves.
meanings.nirukta: parṇa + yat. parṇe bhavaḥ parṇyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in individual leaves, acting as the life force of flora.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'parṇa' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one in the leaf-heaps / dry leaves.
meanings.nirukta: parṇaśada (heap of dry leaves) + yat.
meanings.vedantic: Present in leaf-heaps, representing the cycle of decomposition and rejuvenation.

grammatical_references.panini: Compound 'parṇaśada' + suffix 'yat'. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one raising weapons / brandishing.
meanings.nirukta: apa + gur (to raise/threaten) + śānac. he who threatens the sinners.
meanings.vedantic: The threatening posture of Rudra represents the warning signs of nature and karma, prompting righteousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'apa' + root √gur (udyamane) + middle participle suffix 'śānac' + dative ending.

meanings.english: To the one striking / killing.
meanings.nirukta: abhi + han + śatṛ. he who strikes directly.
meanings.vedantic: The destructive aspect of Rudra that kills the ego and destroys sins.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'abhi' + root √han + active participle 'śatṛ' + dative singular ending 'e'.

meanings.english: To the one afflicting/harassing slightly.
meanings.nirukta: ā + khid (to vex/afflict) + śatṛ. he who causes mild pain.
meanings.vedantic: The minor obstacles and struggles in life are also His grace, helping to develop patience.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'ā' + root √khid + active participle 'śatṛ' with Vedic doubling 'kkh'. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one afflicting/harassing severely.
meanings.nirukta: pra + khid + śatṛ. he who causes intense affliction.
meanings.vedantic: Severe trials and tribulations that lead to deep detachment and spiritual awakening.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'pra' + root √khid + participle 'śatṛ' with Vedic doubling. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the ones who shower wealth/grace or destroy.
meanings.nirukta: kiranti dhanāni iti kirikāḥ (those who scatter wealth).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the bestower of all prosperity (bhukti) and liberation (mukti).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √kṛ (to scatter) + 'ka' suffix with vowel lengthening. Dative plural.

meanings.english: Of the gods.
meanings.nirukta: divyati iti devaḥ (luminous/shining one).
meanings.vedantic: The shining cosmic forces that govern different aspects of creation, all born of the one Supreme.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √div (to shine) + 'ac' suffix. Genitive plural form is 'devānām'.

meanings.english: To the hearts / core.
meanings.nirukta: hṛdi-ayam, the seat of feelings and consciousness.
meanings.vedantic: The innermost core of all beings, where the Self resides and shines.

grammatical_references.panini: Neuter noun 'hṛdaya'. Dative plural 'hṛdayebhyaḥ'.

meanings.english: To the ones who destroy (sin/decay) or are non-decaying.
meanings.nirukta: vi + kṣī (to decay) + suffix. those who do not decay, or who destroy.
meanings.vedantic: The immortal, changeless entities that bring about the destruction of finite forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'vi' + root √kṣī + 'kta' + 'ka' suffix. Dative plural.

meanings.english: To the ones who distinguish (merit and demerit) / investigate.
meanings.nirukta: vi + ci + śatṛ + ka. those who separate good from evil.
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic law of Karma that perfectly differentiates and awards fruits according to actions.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'vi' + root √ci (to gather) + 'śatṛ' + 'ka'. Dative plural.

meanings.english: To the ones who strike down completely / take away.
meanings.nirukta: ā + nihṛ (to strike/punish) + kta. those who carry out divine punishment.
meanings.vedantic: Representing the unavoidable consequences of moral transgression, acting as natural correctives.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefixes 'ā' + 'ni' + root √hṛ + suffix 'kta'. Dative plural.

meanings.english: To the ones who cause diseases to flee, or chase sinners.
meanings.nirukta: ā + mīv (to move/chase) + śatṛ + ka. those who chase away disease/sin.
meanings.vedantic: The healing aspects of Rudra that chase away physical and spiritual diseases from the devotee.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'ā' + root √mīv (to move) + 'śatṛ' + 'ka'. Dative plural.

meanings.english: To the one in the gravelly / stony land.
meanings.nirukta: kutsitāḥ śilāḥ yatra (where there are small, gravelly stones).
meanings.vedantic: Indicates that even in rough, uncomfortable, and small elements of existence, the divine essence resides.

grammatical_references.panini: Compound of 'kim' (vile/small) + 'śilā' (stone) with dative ending 'yāya'.

meanings.english: To the one in the habitable place / dwelling.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'kṣi' meaning to dwell or reside.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the ultimate resting place or abode of all living beings during dissolution.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √kṣi (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0003) + 'lyuṭ' suffix (P.3.3.115) in the sense of location.

meanings.english: To the one standing in front / having straight hair.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'puras' (in front) + root 'as' (to be). He who reveals Himself directly to His devotees.
meanings.vedantic: The ever-present consciousness that stands before everyone as the inner witness.

grammatical_references.panini: Uṇādi-derived noun. Dative singular form is 'pulastaye' by 'gherṅiti' (P.7.3.111).

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Listed under sage names or Shiva's attendants.

meanings.english: To the one situated in the cowshed.
meanings.nirukta: gōṣṭha + yat. gōṣṭhe bhavaḥ gōṣṭhyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the divine presiding over domestic and nurturing spaces, animal shelters, and cattle.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'gōṣṭha' (P.8.3.97) + 'yat' suffix (P.4.3.53) for location.

meanings.english: To the one situated in the house.
meanings.nirukta: gṛha + yat. gṛhe bhavaḥ gṛhyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the Lord as the inner deity of every home, the guardian of the household.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'gṛha' + 'yat' suffix (P.4.3.53) indicating location.

meanings.english: To the one situated on the couch/bed.
meanings.nirukta: talpa + yat. talpe bhavaḥ talpyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in states of rest and sleep, presiding over the state of deep sleep (Suṣupti).

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'talpa' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in the palace/mansion.
meanings.nirukta: geha + yat. gehe bhavaḥ gehyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Present in grand buildings and temples; He occupies both humble homes (gṛha) and grand mansions (geha).

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'geha' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one dwelling in caves/impenetrable clefts.
meanings.nirukta: gahvare tiṣṭhati iti gahvareṣṭhaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: He who resides in the cave of the heart (hṛdaya-guhā) as the ultimate witness.

grammatical_references.panini: Locative compound: 'gahvare' + root √sthā + 'ka' suffix. 'ṣ' substitution by 'ādeśapratyayayoḥ' (P.8.3.59).

meanings.english: To the one situated in deep pools/heart.
meanings.nirukta: hṛda (deep pool) + yat. hṛde bhavaḥ hṛdayyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Presiding over deep waters, representing the coolness of divine grace, and the depth of the heart.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'hṛda' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in dewdrops/mist/snow.
meanings.nirukta: nivēṣpa (mist/fog/dew) + yat. niveṣpe bhavaḥ niveṣpyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in transient and subtle elements like dew, representing the impermanence of creation.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'niveṣpa' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in the fine dust/particles.
meanings.nirukta: pāṃsu (dust/atom) + yat. pāṃsuṣu bhavaḥ pāṃsavyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Divinity is present at the atomic and subatomic level, in every speck of dust.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'pāṃsu' + 'yat' suffix (P.4.3.53). Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in visible dust/clouds.
meanings.nirukta: rajas (dust/sky/passion) + yat. rajasi bhavaḥ rajasyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Presides over the active, passionate quality of nature (Rajas) and the atmosphere.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'rajas' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in dry wood/vegetation.
meanings.nirukta: śuṣka + yat. śuṣke bhavaḥ śuṣkyaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in dry, dead, or decaying matter, showing His role as the destroyer/recycler of life.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'śuṣka' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: To the one situated in green wood/fresh plants.
meanings.nirukta: harita + yat. harite bhavaḥ harityaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is present in vibrant, living, green foliage and fresh life, representing growth.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'harita' + 'yat' suffix. Dative singular.

meanings.english: O driver away of sin / O cloak of the universe.
meanings.nirukta: drapayati pāpakṛtaḥ iti drāpiḥ (he who makes sinners run or suffer). Or a golden mantle.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord acts as a protective cloak for the cosmos, and also drives away the cycle of Samsara.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √drā (to run/flee) + 'pic' + 'in' suffix. Vocative singular.

meanings.english: Of food / Soma juice.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'ad' (to eat) or 'nam' (to flow). That which nourishes.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is the food (Soma) that sustains both physical body and spiritual devotion.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √ad + suffix 'asun' with irregular changes. Genitive singular form is 'andhasaḥ'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under food synonyms (anna-nāmāni, Nighaṇṭu 2.7, item 9) and Soma juice.

meanings.english: O Lord / Protector.
meanings.nirukta: pāti iti patiḥ (he who protects).
meanings.vedantic: The Supreme Ruler and protector of the spiritual nectar (Soma/Andhas).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √pā (to protect) + 'ḍati' suffix (P.3.3.94). Vocative singular.

meanings.english: O non-attached / O poor / O detached one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'daridrā' meaning to be poor. In the case of Shiva, it means He who possesses nothing, hence detached from all worldly items.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the supreme asceticism (Vairāgya) of Lord Shiva, who needs nothing because He is self-complete.

grammatical_references.panini: Intensive form of root √dṛ (to split) or root √daridrā (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0071) + active participle 'śatṛ'. Vocative singular.

meanings.english: O blue-red one.
meanings.nirukta: nīlaśca lohitaśca iti nīlalohitaḥ (having blue neck and red body).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the union of Puruṣa (blue/unmoving) and Prakṛti (red/active), or Śiva and Śakti.

grammatical_references.panini: Karmadhāraya compound: 'nīla' + 'lohita'. Vocative singular.

meanings.english: These / This.
meanings.nirukta: Pronominal base pointing to immediate experience.
meanings.vedantic: The manifest universe which is experienced directly by the senses.

grammatical_references.panini: Pronoun 'idam'. Genitive plural form is 'eṣām' / 'eṣām-puruṣāṇām'.

meanings.english: Of men / people.
meanings.nirukta: puruṣaḥ purīṣādaḥ (dweller of the body-city).
meanings.vedantic: The conscious entities (soul/Jivas) that reside in physical bodies.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √pṛ (to fill) + 'uṣan' suffix. Genitive plural is 'puruṣāṇām'.

meanings.english: Do not fear.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'bhī' meaning to fear.
meanings.vedantic: A request to remove the primordial fear of death and limitation.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √bhī (Juhotyādi-gaṇa, 3.0002). Vedic second-person singular aorist injunctive 'mā bheḥ'.

meanings.english: Do not perish / harm.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'rī' meaning to howl/perish/harm.
meanings.vedantic: A prayer that our cattle, children, and belongings do not suffer destruction.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √rī (Kryādi-gaṇa, 9.0033). Injunctive form 'mā araḥ' / 'mā-aroḥ'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Anything whatsoever.
meanings.nirukta: Indefinite pronoun.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to even the smallest speck of illness or sorrow.

grammatical_references.panini: Compound particle 'kim' + 'ca' + 'na'.

meanings.english: Be diseased / fall ill.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'am' meaning to be sick or afflicted.
meanings.vedantic: A prayer that no disease should strike our people or cattle.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √am (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0507). Vedic injunctive/subjunctive form 'āmamat'.

meanings.english: Auspicious / Benevolent / Source of peace.
meanings.nirukta: śerete asmin sarvam iti śivaḥ (He in whom all things rest).
meanings.vedantic: The non-dual state of pure consciousness which is inherently auspicious and peaceful (Śāntam Śivam Advaitam).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √śī (to sleep/rest) + 'van' suffix. Feminine 'śivā', masculine 'śivaḥ'.

meanings.english: In the highest / supreme.
meanings.nirukta: piparti iti paramaḥ (he who fills or transcends).
meanings.vedantic: The transcendent state of reality, beyond the material plane.

grammatical_references.panini: Adjective 'parama'. Locative singular masculine 'parame'.

meanings.english: Tree.
meanings.nirukta: vraścati iti vṛkṣaḥ (that which is cut down).
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic tree of Samsara (as mentioned in Kathopanishad/Gita), on which the Lord places His weapons.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vraśc (to cut) + suffix 'sa' with irregular changes. Locative singular 'vṛkṣe'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Placing / putting down.
meanings.nirukta: ni + dhā + lyap. placing aside.
meanings.vedantic: Placing aside the weapons signifies the withdrawal of the cosmic dissolution phase.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'ni' + root √dhā + gerund suffix 'lyap' (P.7.1.37).

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Wearing / clad in.
meanings.nirukta: vaste iti vasānaḥ (he who wears).
meanings.vedantic: Wearing the skin represents Shiva's identity as the supreme ascetic.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vas (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0013) + middle participle 'śānac'. Nominative singular 'vasānaḥ'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: The Pināka bow.
meanings.nirukta: pinasti iti pinākam (that which crushes or destroys enemies).
meanings.vedantic: Shiva's divine bow, which represents the cosmic laws that crush ignorance.

grammatical_references.panini: Uṇādi-derived noun. Accusative singular 'pinākam'.

meanings.english: Bearing / holding.
meanings.nirukta: bibharti iti bibhrat (he who holds).
meanings.vedantic: Bearing the bow Pināka, but keeping it unstrung, showing a state of peaceful readiness.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √bhṛ + active participle 'śatṛ'. Nominative singular masculine is 'bibhrat'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: O showerer / O dispeller (of sin/disease).
meanings.nirukta: vikirati duḥkham iti vikiridaḥ (he who scatters or dispels suffering).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord as the active dispeller of spiritual obstacles and biological ailments.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'vi' + root √kṛ (to scatter) + 'dā' suffix. Vocative singular.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Thousand / countless.
meanings.nirukta: sahasra-saṅkhyāka (possessing a thousand or infinite count).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the infinite nature of the Divine manifestations.

grammatical_references.panini: Numeral noun. Accusative singular 'sahasram', nominative plural 'sahasrāņi'.

meanings.english: Weapon / missile.
meanings.nirukta: hinoti iti hetiḥ (that which is thrown to kill/strike).
meanings.vedantic: The weapon of Rudra represents the forces of time and karma that strike down finitude.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √hi (to throw) + suffix 'ktin' (P.3.3.94). Nominative singular 'hetiḥ', nominative plural 'hetayaḥ'.

meanings.english: Other than / someone else.
meanings.nirukta: Indefinite pronominal base denoting difference.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the enemies of Dharma, or the ignorance that is external to the Self.

grammatical_references.panini: Pronoun 'anya'. Accusative singular masculine 'anyam'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Thousand / countless.
meanings.nirukta: sahasra-saṅkhyāka (possessing a thousand or infinite count).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the infinite nature of the Divine manifestations.

grammatical_references.panini: Numeral noun. Accusative singular 'sahasram', nominative plural 'sahasrāņi'.

meanings.english: On the earth.
meanings.nirukta: bhavanti bhūtāni asyām iti bhūmiḥ (the ground where all beings exist).
meanings.vedantic: The physical plane of existence where Jivas undergo their karmic evolution.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'bhūmi'. Locative singular is 'bhūmyām'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: At a distance of a thousand yojanas (leagues).
meanings.nirukta: sahasre yojane (at a distance of 1000 yojanas).
meanings.vedantic: Praying that the weapons of dissolution remain infinitely far away from the devotee.

grammatical_references.panini: Locative compound: 'sahasra' + 'yojana' (a measure of 8-9 miles). Locative singular.

meanings.english: Bows.
meanings.nirukta: dhanvati iti dhanva (that which moves or releases arrows).
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic bows of the various Rudras that enforce the laws of karma.

grammatical_references.panini: Vedic noun 'dhanvan'. Accusative plural is 'dhanvāni'.

meanings.english: Unbend / stretch / unstring.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'tan' meaning to stretch or spread.
meanings.vedantic: The unbending of the bow shows He neutralizes the weapons of karmic retribution.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tan (Tanādi-gaṇa, 8.0001). Vedic second-person singular imperative 'tanuṣva', first-person plural 'tanmasi'.

meanings.english: A healer for all days / universal medicine.
meanings.nirukta: viśveṣu ahaḥsu bhēṣajī (healing at all times).
meanings.vedantic: The ultimate remedy for the chronic disease of transmigratory existence (Samsara).

grammatical_references.panini: Compound: 'viśvaha' (always) + 'bhēṣajī' (healer).

meanings.english: O Rudra / Of Rudra.
meanings.nirukta: He who dissolves suffering (rutaṃ drāvayati).
meanings.vedantic: The supreme healer and destroyer of ignorance.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √rud + 'kran' suffix. Vocative 'rudra', dative 'rudrāya', genitive 'rudrasya'.

meanings.english: The healer / medicine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'bhiṣaj' (healer).
meanings.vedantic: The curative power of divine grace.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'bhiṣaj' with feminine suffix 'ṅīṣ'.

meanings.english: Make happy / grant bliss.
meanings.nirukta: mṛḍayati iti (that which delights or pleases).
meanings.vedantic: The granting of the supreme, unalloyed bliss of Brahman (Ānanda).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √mṛḍ (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0028). Imperative second-person singular 'mṛḍa' or 'mṛḍaya'.

meanings.english: For living / for life.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'jīv' meaning to live.
meanings.vedantic: The vital energy (Prana) that animates the physical sheath to allow spiritual practice.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √jīv (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0649). Vedic dative infinitive 'jīvase' (P.3.4.9).

meanings.english: To the strong / mighty one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'tu' meaning to grow or be strong.
meanings.vedantic: The omnipotent force that sustains the entire cosmos.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tu + suffix 'asun' with augment. Dative singular form is 'tavase'.

meanings.english: To the one whose heroes/servants are ruled or destroyed.
meanings.nirukta: kṣayantaḥ vīrāḥ yasya saḥ (under whose command all heroes/energies stand). Or he who destroys disease/sin.
meanings.vedantic: The Supreme Ruler (Iśvara) who governs all active cosmic forces and reduces the ego-heroes to insignificance.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound: 'kṣayat' (ruling/ruling over) + 'vīra' (hero). Dative singular.

meanings.english: Bearing / holding.
meanings.nirukta: bibharti iti bibhrat (he who holds).
meanings.vedantic: Bearing the bow Pināka, but keeping it unstrung, showing a state of peaceful readiness.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √bhṛ + active participle 'śatṛ'. Nominative singular masculine is 'bibhrat'.

meanings.english: Intellect / prayer / meditation.
meanings.nirukta: manyate anayā iti matiḥ (that by which we think or contemplate).
meanings.vedantic: The refined intellect (Buddhi) directed toward the meditation on Brahman.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √man + suffix 'ktin' (P.3.3.94). Accusative singular is 'matim'.

meanings.english: Peace / welfare / spiritual calm.
meanings.nirukta: śamayati duḥkham iti śam (that which quietens or pacifies suffering).
meanings.vedantic: The quietude of the mind (Śama) which is a pre-requisite for Self-realization.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √śam (to be calm) + 'kvip' suffix. Neuter singular.

meanings.english: To the two-legged beings (humans).
meanings.nirukta: dvau pādau yasya tat dvipadam (possessing two feet).
meanings.vedantic: Refers to human Jivas, who require peace of mind to progress on the path of evolution.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound: 'dvi' + 'pāda' (P.5.4.140). Dative singular 'dvipade'.

meanings.english: To the four-legged beings (animals).
meanings.nirukta: catvāraḥ pādāḥ yasya tat catuṣpadam.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to animals, who also require health and freedom from fear under the Lord's governance.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound: 'catur' + 'pāda'. Dative singular 'catuṣpade'.

meanings.english: All / Entire / Universe.
meanings.nirukta: viśati iti viśvam (that which is entered, or enters).
meanings.vedantic: The entire cosmos, which is entered and pervaded by the omnipresent Brahman.

grammatical_references.panini: Sarvanāma (pronoun) base meaning 'all'. Neuter singular 'viśvam'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Nānārthavarga: 'viśvam jagati ca triṣu' (defining viśva as world or all).

meanings.english: Nourished / prosperous.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'puṣ' meaning to nourish or thrive.
meanings.vedantic: The state of complete physical and spiritual enrichment resulting from divine alignment.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √puṣ (Divādi-gaṇa, 4.0084) + suffix 'kta'. Neuter singular 'puṣṭam'.

meanings.english: In the village.
meanings.nirukta: gṛhyate bhogaḥ atra iti grāmaḥ (where worldly enjoyment is gathered).
meanings.vedantic: The social environment or community of Jivas living together.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √grah + 'ghañ' with irregular changes. Locative singular is 'grāme'.

meanings.english: These / This.
meanings.nirukta: Pronominal base pointing to immediate experience.
meanings.vedantic: The manifest universe which is experienced directly by the senses.

grammatical_references.panini: Pronoun 'idam'. Genitive plural form is 'eṣām' / 'eṣām-puruṣāṇām'.

meanings.english: Healthy / free from illness.
meanings.nirukta: na āturaḥ iti anāturaḥ (not sick/unafflicted).
meanings.vedantic: The state of physical health and mental stability required to undertake spiritual enquiry.

grammatical_references.panini: Nañ-tatpuruṣa compound: negative prefix 'an' + adjective 'ātura' (diseased).

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Bliss / happiness / spiritual joy.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'mī' meaning to delight, or from 'ma' (bliss).
meanings.vedantic: The essential nature of the Self, which is Anandamaya (made of bliss).

grammatical_references.panini: Vedic noun 'mayas' ending in 'as'. Accusative singular.

meanings.english: Make / grant.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'kṛ' meaning to do or make.
meanings.vedantic: The request to actively bestow peace and welfare upon us.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √kṛ (Tanādi-gaṇa, 8.0010). Vedic imperative second-person singular 'kṛdhi' (P.6.4.101).

meanings.english: Salutations / Bowing down / Reverence.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the root 'nam' (नम्) meaning 'to bow, bend, or submit'. In Nirukta, 'namas' is defined in the sense of bowing or honoring.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the dissolution of ego ('Na' = not, 'mama' = mine). It is the surrender of the individual self into the supreme, cosmic self.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived from root √nam (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1030) with the suffix 'asun'. Governs the dative case by 'Namaḥ-svasti-svāhā...' (P.2.3.16).

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under water synonyms (Nighaṇṭu 1.12) and food synonyms (Nighaṇṭu 2.7).

meanings.english: May we worship / serve.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'vidh' meaning to worship, honor, or serve.
meanings.vedantic: Worship is the active alignment of our mind and actions with the cosmic order.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vidh (Adādi-gaṇa) or √vidh (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0964). Vedic optative first-person plural 'vidhema'.

meanings.english: Which / Who.
meanings.nirukta: Relative pronoun base.
meanings.vedantic: Points to the ultimate substrate that manifests as the active forces of nature.

grammatical_references.panini: Relative pronoun 'yad'. Feminine singular form is 'yā'.

meanings.english: Manu (the progenitor of mankind).
meanings.nirukta: manyate iti manuḥ (the thinking being, progenitor of humans).
meanings.vedantic: The archetypal mind that sets the code of conduct (Dharma) for the current age.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √man + suffix 'u'. Nominative singular is 'manuḥ'.

meanings.english: Worshipped / sacrificed.
meanings.nirukta: ā + yaj (to worship). he who attained the fruits of sacrifice.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the ideal performer of Yajña (sacrifice) whose path we seek to follow.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'ā' + root √yaj + Lat/Liṭ first-person singular middle 'ā-yaje'.

meanings.english: Father / progenitor.
meanings.nirukta: pātā vā pālayitā vā pitā (he who protects or nourishes).
meanings.vedantic: The source of physical lineage; also represents the lineage of teachers (Guru-paramparā).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √pā + suffix 'tṛc' with augment. Nominative singular 'pitā', accusative 'pitaram'.

meanings.english: That / By that.
meanings.nirukta: Demonstrative pronoun base.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the transcendent reality that is experienced as the substrate of all.

grammatical_references.panini: Pronoun 'tad'. Instrumental 'tayā', accusative 'tat'.

meanings.english: May we attain / enjoy.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'aṃś' or 'aś' meaning to reach or enjoy.
meanings.vedantic: The prayer to experience the supreme fruit of our efforts under the Lord's guidance.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √aś (Kryādi-gaṇa, 9.0019) or √aṃś. Vedic optative first-person plural 'aśyāma' (P.3.4.116).

meanings.english: Under the guidance / love.
meanings.nirukta: pra + nī + kti. pranayana (guidance/love/lead).
meanings.vedantic: Living in complete harmony with the divine plan, led by the Guru's guidance.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'pra' + root √nī + suffix 'ktin'. Locative singular is 'praṇītau'.

meanings.english: The elder / great / large.
meanings.nirukta: mahate iti mahān (that which is expanded or great).
meanings.vedantic: The elder generation, or the vast and great elements of creation.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √mah + suffix 'atup'. Accusative singular masculine 'mahāntam'.

meanings.english: The child / small / young.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'arbh' meaning to kill or diminish. That which is small.
meanings.vedantic: The young, vulnerable, or underdeveloped elements of our community and inner self.

grammatical_references.panini: Uṇādi-derived noun. Accusative singular masculine 'arbhakam'.

meanings.english: The growing youth / procreant.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'ukṣ' meaning to sprinkle, grow, or strengthen.
meanings.vedantic: The active, productive force of youth that builds and sprinks vital energy.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √ukṣ (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0747) + participle 'śatṛ'. Accusative singular 'ukṣantam'.

meanings.english: The infant / womb-born / fully grown.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'ukṣ' + kta. that which is sprinkled or fully formed.
meanings.vedantic: The unborn child in the womb, or the child that has just been born.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √ukṣ + suffix 'kta'. Accusative singular 'ukṣitam'.

meanings.english: Do not kill / destroy.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'vadh' meaning to strike, kill, or destroy.
meanings.vedantic: A prayer for the preservation of life and the avoidance of untimely death (Apamṛtyu).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vadh (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1047). Vedic aorist injunctive second-person singular 'vadhīḥ'.

meanings.english: Father / progenitor.
meanings.nirukta: pātā vā pālayitā vā pitā (he who protects or nourishes).
meanings.vedantic: The source of physical lineage; also represents the lineage of teachers (Guru-paramparā).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √pā + suffix 'tṛc' with augment. Nominative singular 'pitā', accusative 'pitaram'.

meanings.english: Father / progenitor.
meanings.nirukta: pātā vā pālayitā vā pitā (he who protects or nourishes).
meanings.vedantic: The source of physical lineage; also represents the lineage of teachers (Guru-paramparā).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √pā + suffix 'tṛc' with augment. Nominative singular 'pitā', accusative 'pitaram'.

meanings.english: Dear / beloved ones.
meanings.nirukta: prīṇati iti priyaḥ (that which pleases or is loved).
meanings.vedantic: The objects and people that are dear to us, reflecting the innate love (Premo) of the Self.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √prī + suffix 'ka' (P.3.1.135). Accusative plural feminine 'priyāḥ'.

meanings.english: Body / Manifest form.
meanings.nirukta: tanoti iti tanūḥ (that which stretches or manifests).
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic body of the Lord, which is the entire universe, and His subtle body of knowledge.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tan (to stretch) + 'ū' suffix. Nominative singular 'tanūḥ', nominative plural 'tanuvaḥ'.

meanings.english: Do not injure / harm.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'riṣ' meaning to hurt or injure.
meanings.vedantic: A prayer that no harm should come to our spiritual bodies and physical frames.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √riṣ (Divādi-gaṇa, 4.0141). Vedic aorist injunctive second-person singular 'rīriṣaḥ'.

meanings.english: Children / progeny.
meanings.nirukta: tudyate anena pitroḥ manaḥ iti tokaḥ (that which delights parents).
meanings.vedantic: The future generation, representing the expansion of the family line.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived noun. Locative singular 'toke', dative 'tokāya'.

meanings.english: Grandchildren / sons.
meanings.nirukta: tanoti pitroḥ kulam iti tanayaḥ (he who stretches or continues the family).
meanings.vedantic: The continuation of life through successive generations.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tan + suffix 'kayan'. Locative singular 'tanaye', dative 'tanayāya'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Cows / cattle.
meanings.nirukta: gacchati iti gauḥ (that which moves/walks).
meanings.vedantic: The sacred, nurturing elements of nature (represented by cows), which provide milk and sustain life.

grammatical_references.panini: Irregular noun 'go'. Locative plural is 'gōṣu'.

meanings.english: Horses / transport.
meanings.nirukta: aśnute adhvānam iti aśvaḥ (he who eats or covers the path).
meanings.vedantic: Represent the senses and the power of transportation/speed in the world.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √aś (to reach) + suffix 'kvan' (Uṇādi 1.149). Locative plural 'aśveṣu'.

meanings.english: Heroes / brave warriors / brave servants.
meanings.nirukta: vīrayate iti vīraḥ (he who exhibits valor).
meanings.vedantic: The inner strength and qualities of courage required to battle spiritual ignorance.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vīr (to be brave) + suffix 'ac'. Accusative plural is 'vīrān'.

meanings.english: Being angry / wrathful.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'bhām' meaning to be angry.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the protective and corrective anger of Rudra, which destroys evil.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √bhām + suffix 'kta' + suffix 'tas' (Vedic instrumental adverb).

meanings.english: Those offering oblations.
meanings.nirukta: havis (oblation) + matup. those who perform sacrifices.
meanings.vedantic: The dedicated practitioners who perform outer and inner sacrifices to the Divine.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'havis' + possessive suffix 'matup'. Nominative plural masculine is 'haviṣmantaḥ'.

meanings.english: From a distance / far away.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from 'āra' (distance).
meanings.vedantic: A prayer that the destructive aspects of Rudra remain far from us.

grammatical_references.panini: Ablative singular form of 'āra' used as an indeclinable adverb.

meanings.english: To the cow-killing weapon / cow-slayer.
meanings.nirukta: gāṃ hanti iti goghnaḥ (that which strikes cows).
meanings.vedantic: The potential calamity or disease affecting our cattle; we pray it remains far away.

grammatical_references.panini: Compound: 'go' + root √han + suffix 'ka' (P.3.2.54). Dative singular 'goghne'.

meanings.english: To the man-killing weapon / man-slayer.
meanings.nirukta: puruṣaṃ hanti iti puruṣaghnaḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The potential disasters or epidemics affecting humans; we pray they avoid us.

grammatical_references.panini: Compound: 'puruṣa' + root √han + 'ka'. Dative singular 'puruṣaghne'.

meanings.english: Grace / benevolence / happiness.
meanings.nirukta: su + manaḥ = sumanas. that which makes the mind pleasant.
meanings.vedantic: The peaceful state of mind that arises through divine benevolence.

grammatical_references.panini: Vedic noun 'sumna' derived from prefix 'su' + root √man + 'ka'. Nominative singular.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Protect us.
meanings.nirukta: rakṣati iti (that which keeps safe).
meanings.vedantic: The divine shield that protects the seeker from internal and external obstacles.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √rakṣ (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.0743). Imperative second-person singular 'rakṣa'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Speak (for us) / advocate.
meanings.nirukta: brūte iti (he who speaks or declares).
meanings.vedantic: Addressing the Lord as our advocate, who speaks words of favor to the higher cosmic forces.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √brū (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0039). Imperative second-person singular 'brūhi'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Protection / shelter / happiness.
meanings.nirukta: śaraṇaṃ bhavatīti śarma (that which provides refuge).
meanings.vedantic: The ultimate refuge of the Jiva, which is the non-dual consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √śṛ + suffix 'manin'. Accusative singular is 'śarma'.

lexicographical_references.nighantu: Listed under house/refuge synonyms (Nighaṇṭu 3.4) and happiness (3.6).

meanings.english: Grant / bestow.
meanings.nirukta: yacchati iti (he who gives or binds).
meanings.vedantic: The request to bestow the peace and refuge we seek.
