grammatical_references.panini: Root √yam (Bhvādi-gaṇa, 1.1137). Imperative second-person singular 'yaccha'.

meanings.english: Dwelling in two worlds / having double strength.
meanings.nirukta: dvayoḥ sthānayoḥ bṛhantaḥ (mighty in both earth and heaven).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord who is both transcendent (heaven/unmanifest) and immanent (earth/manifest).

grammatical_references.panini: Vedic compound: 'dvi' + 'barhas' (mighty/expanded). Nominative singular.

meanings.english: Praise.
meanings.nirukta: stauti iti (he who glorifies).
meanings.vedantic: Praise is the vocal expression of our devotion, focusing the mind on divine qualities.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √stu (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0042). Imperative second-person singular 'stuhi'.

meanings.english: The famous / renowned one.
meanings.nirukta: śrūyate iti śrutaḥ (he who is heard of through Vedic texts).
meanings.vedantic: The Supreme Brahman who is revealed through the Upanishadic texts (Śrutis).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √śru (to hear) + suffix 'kta'. Accusative singular is 'śrutam'.

meanings.english: Sitting in the chariot/heart cave.
meanings.nirukta: garte (chariot-seat or cave of the heart) sīdati iti gartasad.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord who resides in the intellect (buddhi-guhā) as the ultimate witness.

grammatical_references.panini: Locative compound: 'garte' + root √sad + 'kvip' suffix. Accusative singular 'gartasadam'.

meanings.english: The ever-youthful.
meanings.nirukta: yauti iti yuvā (he who joins or possesses strength).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is changeless; He never decays or ages, being ever-youthful.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'yuvan'. Accusative singular masculine is 'yuvānam'.

meanings.english: The wild beast / lion.
meanings.nirukta: mṛgyate iti mṛgaḥ (that which is hunted or seeks). In context, a fierce beast like a lion.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord is like a majestic lion, representing supreme strength, independence, and fearlessness.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √mṛg (to seek) + suffix 'ac'. Accusative singular 'mṛgam'.

meanings.english: Us / Our.
meanings.nirukta: First-person pronoun base.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the collective assembly of devotees praying together.

grammatical_references.panini: Pronoun 'asmad'. Dative/accusative plural enclitic 'naḥ', locative 'asme'.

meanings.english: Fearsome / awesome.
meanings.nirukta: bibheti asmāt iti bhīmaḥ (he from whom everyone fears).
meanings.vedantic: The awesome majesty of the Infinite, which causes the laws of nature to function in fear (bhayādasyāgniḥ...).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √bhī + suffix 'makan' (Uṇādi 1.144). Accusative singular 'bhīmam'.

meanings.english: The destroyer / striker.
meanings.nirukta: upa + han + knu. he who strikes from close quarters.
meanings.vedantic: The imminent death or corrective force that stands near to terminate our illusions.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'upa' + root √han + suffix 'knu' (P.3.2.140). Accusative singular 'upahatnum'.

meanings.english: The fierce / mighty one.
meanings.nirukta: ucchyate iti ugraḥ (he who is high, mighty, or terrible).
meanings.vedantic: The terrifying majesty of the ultimate reality when experienced through the ego.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √as (to be) or √uc + 'ran' suffix with irregular changes. Accusative singular 'ugram'.

meanings.english: To the singer / worshipper.
meanings.nirukta: gṛṇāti stuti-rūpeṇa iti jaritā (he who sings hymns).
meanings.vedantic: The devotee who expresses his inner communion through sacred chants.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √gṛ (to sing) + suffix 'tṛc' with Vedic changes. Dative singular 'jaritre'.

meanings.english: Being praised.
meanings.nirukta: stūyamānaḥ (he who is currently being glorified).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord who is ever-worshipped through all speech and hymns in the cosmos.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √stu + passive/middle participle suffix 'śānac'. Nominative singular 'stuvānaḥ'.

meanings.english: Armies / forces.
meanings.nirukta: sināti iti senā (that which binds or is bound together).
meanings.vedantic: The active forces of nature and karma under the command of the Lord.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √si (to bind) + 'na' suffix. Nominative plural 'senāḥ'.

meanings.english: Of the glowing / fierce one.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'tviṣ' meaning to shine or glow.
meanings.vedantic: The brilliant, blinding light of truth that is terrifying to the ego.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tviṣ (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0069) + 'ghañ' suffix. Genitive singular masculine 'tveṣasya'.

meanings.english: Ill-will / anger / bad intellect.
meanings.nirukta: duṣṭā matiḥ (corrupted thinking/will).
meanings.vedantic: The terrifying aspect of karma that appears as the wrath of the universe.

grammatical_references.panini: Prādi-bahuvrīhi compound: 'dus' + 'mati'. Nominative singular 'durmatiḥ'.

meanings.english: Of the sinful / desiring evil.
meanings.nirukta: aghaṃ (sin) icchati iti aghayuḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Those forces or entities that wish harm upon the righteous.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'agha' + denominative suffix 'kyac' + 'u'. Genitive plural 'aghayūnām' or genitive singular 'aghayōḥ'.

meanings.english: Firm / strung.
meanings.nirukta: tiṣṭhati iti sthiraḥ (that which stands firm).
meanings.vedantic: The firm, ready bow of Rudra represents the ever-ready cosmic justice.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √sthā + 'ka' suffix. Accusative plural feminine 'sthiraḥ' / 'sthira' (Vedic).

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Unbend / stretch / unstring.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'tan' meaning to stretch or spread.
meanings.vedantic: The unbending of the bow shows He neutralizes the weapons of karmic retribution.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tan (Tanādi-gaṇa, 8.0001). Vedic second-person singular imperative 'tanuṣva', first-person plural 'tanmasi'.

meanings.english: O showerer of grace / most bountiful.
meanings.nirukta: miḍhati (showers) iti mīḍhvān.
meanings.vedantic: The Lord who showers bliss and material prosperity on the devotees.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √mih + Vedic suffix 'kvasu'. Vocative 'mīḍhvaḥ', superlative vocative 'mīḍhuṣṭama'.

meanings.english: Children / progeny.
meanings.nirukta: tudyate anena pitroḥ manaḥ iti tokaḥ (that which delights parents).
meanings.vedantic: The future generation, representing the expansion of the family line.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived noun. Locative singular 'toke', dative 'tokāya'.

meanings.english: Grandchildren / sons.
meanings.nirukta: tanoti pitroḥ kulam iti tanayaḥ (he who stretches or continues the family).
meanings.vedantic: The continuation of life through successive generations.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tan + suffix 'kayan'. Locative singular 'tanaye', dative 'tanayāya'.

meanings.english: The elder / great / large.
meanings.nirukta: mahate iti mahān (that which is expanded or great).
meanings.vedantic: The elder generation, or the vast and great elements of creation.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √mah + suffix 'atup'. Accusative singular masculine 'mahāntam'.

meanings.english: In the ocean.
meanings.nirukta: arṇasaḥ (of water) samūhaḥ arṇavaḥ (the ocean or great water-mass).
meanings.vedantic: The vast ocean of Samsara, or the cosmic waters of creation.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'arṇas' (water) + 'va' suffix (P.5.2.109). Locative singular 'arṇave'.

meanings.english: In the atmosphere / intermediate region.
meanings.nirukta: antarā ikṣyate iti antarikṣam (that which is seen between heaven and earth).
meanings.vedantic: The subtle plane of existence (Prāṇamaya/Manomaya sheaths).

grammatical_references.panini: Locative compound. Locative singular 'antarikṣe'.

meanings.english: Bhavas / Rudras (manifestations of Shiva).
meanings.nirukta: bhavanti bhūtāni asmāt iti bhavaḥ (the source of all existence).
meanings.vedantic: The aspect of Shiva that brings forth the manifest universe; here addressed in the plural.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √bhū + 'ghañ' suffix. Nominative plural 'bhavāḥ'.

lexicographical_references.amara_kosha: Svargavarga: Shiva's name: 'bhavaḥ śarvo haro rudraḥ'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: In heaven.
meanings.nirukta: divyati iti dyuḥ (the shining sky or heavenly plane).
meanings.vedantic: The causal plane of existence (Kāraṇa-śarīra / Svar-loka).

grammatical_references.panini: Consonant noun 'div'. Locative singular 'divi', accusative 'divam'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Wind / atmosphere.
meanings.nirukta: vāti iti vātaḥ (that which blows).
meanings.vedantic: The vital air (Prana) that regulates the breath of the cosmos.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vā (to blow) + 'tan' suffix with augment. Nominative singular 'vātaḥ'.

meanings.english: Rain / rainfall.
meanings.nirukta: varṣati iti varṣam (that which pours or rains).
meanings.vedantic: The rainfall that nourishes the earth, acting as the blessing of the heavens.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vṛṣ (to rain) + 'ghañ' suffix. Nominative/Accusative singular 'varṣam'.

meanings.english: Ten / ten fingers/salutations.
meanings.nirukta: daśan-saṅkhyāka.
meanings.vedantic: Representing the ten directions, or full surrender with all ten fingers folded.

grammatical_references.panini: Numeral 'daśan'. Nominative plural 'daśa'.

meanings.english: East / eastward-facing.
meanings.nirukta: prāñc (forward/east).
meanings.vedantic: Facing the rising sun, representing the dawn of wisdom.

grammatical_references.panini: Feminine form of 'prāñc'. Accusative plural feminine 'prācīḥ'.

meanings.english: South / southward-facing.
meanings.nirukta: dakṣiṇa (right side or south).
meanings.vedantic: Facing the south, the direction of Yama (death/justice).

grammatical_references.panini: Feminine form of 'dakṣiṇa'. Accusative plural 'dakṣiṇāḥ'.

meanings.english: West / westward-facing.
meanings.nirukta: pratyac (backward/west).
meanings.vedantic: Facing the west, representing the setting sun and reflection.

grammatical_references.panini: Feminine form of 'pratyac'. Accusative plural 'pratīcīḥ'.

meanings.english: North / northward-facing.
meanings.nirukta: udañc (upward/north).
meanings.vedantic: Facing the north, the direction of Shiva (Himalayas) and liberation.

grammatical_references.panini: Feminine form of 'udañc'. Accusative plural 'udīcīḥ'.

meanings.english: Upward / upward-facing.
meanings.nirukta: ūrdhva (above/zenith).
meanings.vedantic: Facing the zenith, representing the transcendent realm of pure consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Feminine form of 'ūrdhva'. Accusative plural 'ūrdhvāḥ'.

meanings.english: Make happy / grant bliss.
meanings.nirukta: mṛḍayati iti (that which delights or pleases).
meanings.vedantic: The granting of the supreme, unalloyed bliss of Brahman (Ānanda).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √mṛḍ (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0028). Imperative second-person singular 'mṛḍa' or 'mṛḍaya'.

meanings.english: Which / Who.
meanings.nirukta: Relative pronoun base.
meanings.vedantic: Points to the ultimate substrate that manifests as the active forces of nature.

grammatical_references.panini: Relative pronoun 'yad'. Feminine singular form is 'yā'.

meanings.english: We hate.
meanings.nirukta: dveṣṭi iti dveṣaḥ (he who hates).
meanings.vedantic: The lower emotions of hatred and duality that must be surrendered.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √dviṣ (Adādi-gaṇa, 2.0004). Lat first-person plural 'dviṣmaḥ' / 'dviṣmaḥ'.

meanings.english: Hates.
meanings.nirukta: dveṣaḥ (aversion/hatred).
meanings.vedantic: Those external entities or internal impulses that exhibit aversion to the good.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √dviṣ. Lat third-person singular 'dveṣṭi'.

meanings.english: That / By that.
meanings.nirukta: Demonstrative pronoun base.
meanings.vedantic: Refers to the transcendent reality that is experienced as the substrate of all.

grammatical_references.panini: Pronoun 'tad'. Instrumental 'tayā', accusative 'tat'.

meanings.english: In the open mouth / jaws.
meanings.nirukta: jambhante dantaḥ atra iti jambhaḥ (the jaws or gaping mouth).
meanings.vedantic: The devouring mouth of cosmic time (Kāla), where all dualities and enemies are consumed.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √jabh + 'ghañ'. Locative singular is 'jambhe'.

meanings.english: I place / surrender.
meanings.nirukta: dadhāti iti (he who places or holds).
meanings.vedantic: Placing the enemy of our soul (ego) into the jaws of cosmic time, releasing ourselves.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √dhā (Juhotyādi-gaṇa, 3.0010). Lat first-person singular 'dadhāmi'.

meanings.english: Blue-necked ones.
meanings.nirukta: nīlā grīvā yeṣāṃ te nīlagrīvāḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Those who contain the poison of worldliness in their throats, saving the universe.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound: 'nīla' + 'grīvā'. Nominative plural masculine 'nīlagrīvāḥ'.

meanings.english: White-necked ones / dark-necked.
meanings.nirukta: śitiḥ (white or dark blue) kaṇṭhaḥ yeṣāṃ te śitikaṇṭhāḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The neck region showing both the light of pure consciousness and the dark spot of poison.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound: 'śiti' + 'kaṇṭha'. Nominative plural masculine 'śitikaṇṭhāḥ'.

meanings.english: Sharvas / destroyers.
meanings.nirukta: śṛṇāti iti śarvaḥ (he who destroys or injures evil).
meanings.vedantic: The aspect of Shiva that destroys the finite creation during dissolution.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √śṛ + 'van' suffix with Vedic changes. Nominative plural 'śarvāḥ'.

meanings.english: Below / in the lower regions.
meanings.nirukta: Vedic indeclinable indicating downward direction.
meanings.vedantic: Presiding over the nether worlds (Pātāla, etc.) and the lower energy centers (Mūlādhāra).

grammatical_references.panini: Avyaya (adverb).

meanings.english: Roaming on the earth / ground.
meanings.nirukta: kṣamāyāṃ (on earth) caranti iti kṣamācarāḥ.
meanings.vedantic: Those spiritual forces that walk on the physical plane to protect seekers.

grammatical_references.panini: Locative compound: 'kṣamā' + root √car + 'a' suffix. Nominative plural 'kṣamācarāḥ'.

meanings.english: In heaven.
meanings.nirukta: divyati iti dyuḥ (the shining sky or heavenly plane).
meanings.vedantic: The causal plane of existence (Kāraṇa-śarīra / Svar-loka).

grammatical_references.panini: Consonant noun 'div'. Locative singular 'divi', accusative 'divam'.

meanings.english: Residing in / taking refuge.
meanings.nirukta: upa + ā + śri + kta. those who have taken seat in.
meanings.vedantic: The various deities and powers that reside in the celestial spheres.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefixes 'upa' + 'ā' + root √śri + suffix 'kta'. Nominative plural 'upāśritāḥ'.

meanings.english: Tree.
meanings.nirukta: vraścati iti vṛkṣaḥ (that which is cut down).
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic tree of Samsara (as mentioned in Kathopanishad/Gita), on which the Lord places His weapons.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √vraśc (to cut) + suffix 'sa' with irregular changes. Locative singular 'vṛkṣe'.

meanings.english: Golden-yellow / yellow-green like young grass.
meanings.nirukta: śaspa (young grass) + piñjara (golden-yellow).
meanings.vedantic: The Lord's body glowing like fresh vegetation, representing growth and vital force.

grammatical_references.panini: Compound: 'śaspa' + 'piñjara' with Vedic vowel change. Nominative plural masculine 'saspiñjarāḥ'.

meanings.english: To the Lord / Attribute of the Divine.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from the respective Sanskrit verbal root.
meanings.vedantic: Represents the omnipresent consciousness manifesting in all forms.

grammatical_references.panini: Derived with appropriate grammatical suffix and case ending.

meanings.english: Of spirits / living beings.
meanings.nirukta: bhavanti bhūtāni (those who have come to exist).
meanings.vedantic: All creatures, including invisible spirits and elemental forces, ruled by Shiva (Bhūtānātha).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √bhū + suffix 'kta'. Genitive plural is 'bhūtānām'.

meanings.english: Lords / rulers.
meanings.nirukta: adhikaḥ patiḥ adhipatiḥ (supreme master).
meanings.vedantic: The Lords of the elemental spirits, governing under the supreme command.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'adhi' + noun 'pati'. Nominative plural is 'adhipatayaḥ'.

meanings.english: Shaven-headed / without top-knot.
meanings.nirukta: viśatā śikhā yasya saḥ (without a śikhā/hair).
meanings.vedantic: Represents the sannyāsa state (ascetic monks), who have shaved their heads.

grammatical_references.panini: Bahuvrīhi compound: 'vi' (devoid of) + 'śikhā' (top-knot). Nominative plural masculine 'viśikhāsaḥ'.

meanings.english: In the foods.
meanings.nirukta: adyate iti annam (that which is eaten by living beings).
meanings.vedantic: The physical food that sustains the body; He is present as the digestive power (Vaiśvānara).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √ad + suffix 'kta' with irregular changes (P.8.2.42). Locative plural 'anneṣu'.

meanings.english: They pierce / strike.
meanings.nirukta: vividhyanti (strike or pierce in various ways).
meanings.vedantic: Represent the diseases and imbalances that afflict the body due to wrong eating.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'vi' + root √vyadh (to pierce, Divādi-gaṇa, 4.0078). Lat third-person plural 'vividhyanti'.

meanings.english: In the vessels / drinking cups.
meanings.nirukta: pīyate asmin iti pātram (that in which liquids are drunk).
meanings.vedantic: The vessels of life; He is present in the liquids we consume (milk, water).

grammatical_references.panini: Root √pā (to drink) + suffix 'ṣṭran' (P.3.2.182). Locative plural 'pātreṣu'.

meanings.english: Drinking / those who drink.
meanings.nirukta: pibati iti piban (he who drinks).
meanings.vedantic: Addressing the Jivas while they consume water or milk; He is the consumer as well.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √pā + active participle 'śatṛ'. Accusative plural masculine 'pibataḥ'.

meanings.english: People / living beings.
meanings.nirukta: jāyante iti janāḥ (those who are born).
meanings.vedantic: The collection of individual Jivas in the world.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √jan (to be born) + suffix 'ac'. Accusative plural 'janān'.

meanings.english: Of the paths / roads.
meanings.nirukta: panthāḥ (roads for traveling).
meanings.vedantic: The paths of action (Karma-mārga) and knowledge (Jñāna-mārga).

grammatical_references.panini: Consonant noun 'pathin'. Genitive plural 'pathām'.

meanings.english: The protectors of the paths.
meanings.nirukta: pathām rakṣinaḥ (those who guard the paths).
meanings.vedantic: Those spiritual guardians who protect the path of righteousness from evil obstacles.

grammatical_references.panini: Compound: 'pathi' + root √rakṣ + 'ini' suffix. Nominative plural 'pathirakṣayaḥ'.

meanings.english: The food-givers / strength-bearers.
meanings.nirukta: elām (food/strength) bibhrati iti ailabṛdāḥ.
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic forces that provide physical food and vital energy to the travelers.

grammatical_references.panini: Vedic derivative compound. Nominative plural 'ailabṛdāḥ'.

meanings.english: The fighters / repellers of sinners.
meanings.nirukta: yauvanti papinaḥ iti yavyudhaḥ (those who fight and repel the sinful).
meanings.vedantic: The cosmic protectors who prevent the unworthy from accessing the sacred path.

grammatical_references.panini: Vedic compound. Nominative plural 'yavyudhaḥ'.

meanings.english: The holy fords / water crossings.
meanings.nirukta: taranti anena iti tīrtham (the place where one crosses over a river or Samsara).
meanings.vedantic: The sacred pilgrimage places (Kashi, Prayaga) and the inner crossings of consciousness.

grammatical_references.panini: Root √tṛ (to cross) + Uṇādi suffix 'thak'. Accusative plural 'tīrthāni'.

meanings.english: They roam about / protect.
meanings.nirukta: pra + caranti (they move about diligently).
meanings.vedantic: The active movement of the guardians to ensure the sanctity of pilgrimage places.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'pra' + root √car. Lat third-person plural 'pracaranti'.

meanings.english: Bearing missiles/sharp fangs.
meanings.nirukta: sṛka (missile or fang) + matup. those who hold sharp weapons.
meanings.vedantic: The sharp, cutting edge of cosmic laws that strike down the impure.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'sṛka' + possessive suffix 'matup' (Vedic 'vant'). Nominative plural masculine 'sṛkāvantaḥ'.

meanings.english: Bearing swords / quivers.
meanings.nirukta: niṣaṅgaḥ (quiver/sword) asya asti iti niṣaṅgī.
meanings.vedantic: The well-equipped guardians of truth, representing complete spiritual armament.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'niṣaṅga' + suffix 'ini'. Nominative plural masculine 'niṣaṅgiṇaḥ'.

meanings.english: As many as these.
meanings.nirukta: etad + vat. this many.
meanings.vedantic: Points to the infinite number of Rudras mentioned in the hymn.

grammatical_references.panini: Pronoun base 'etad' + suffix 'vatup'. Nominative plural masculine 'etāvantaḥ'.

meanings.english: Even more than that / more numerous.
meanings.nirukta: Derived from root 'bhū' + iyasun. greater in number.
meanings.vedantic: Indicates that the manifestations of the Divine are beyond any limit or count.

grammatical_references.panini: Comparative suffix 'iyasun' on root √bhū. Nominative plural masculine 'bhūyāṃsaḥ'.

meanings.english: Directions / quarters.
meanings.nirukta: diśanti pradeśān iti diśaḥ (that which points out regions).
meanings.vedantic: The spatial dimensions of the universe, all filled by the Lord.

grammatical_references.panini: Noun 'diś'. Accusative plural is 'diśaḥ'.

meanings.english: They have spread out / pervaded.
meanings.nirukta: vi + tasthire (pervaded all around).
meanings.vedantic: The omnipresence of Rudra, filling all directions of space.

grammatical_references.panini: Prefix 'vi' + root √sthā + Liṭ third-person plural middle 'vi-tasthire'.
